pitfall-reference-table-three-columns.description: Pitfall reference table — three columns (Mistake | Effect | Fix) so the master can scan-and-match against the current dispatch.

archive-then-clear-rule-copy.description: Archive-then-clear rule: copy dashboard to Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/ then `rm -f progress/*.json` — never clear without archiving.

initialization-json-write-once-all.description: initialization.json is write-once — all lifecycle data flows through worker progress files.

plan-then-approval-gate-before.description: Plan-then-approval gate before any dispatch — wasted compute if user rejects the approach.

read-command-file-p-track.description: Read the command file (p_track.md / p.md) in full every invocation — do not work from memory.

first-positional-id-parameter-rule.description: First-positional-{id} parameter rule — non-flag, non-task-id strings are consumed as dashboard IDs (6-char hex, `ide`, or legacy `dashboardN`).

resolution-order-1-pre-assigned.description: Resolution order: (1) pre-assigned via system prompt `DASHBOARD ID:` (highest priority) → (2) explicit --dashboard flag → (no fallback).

archive-then-clear-then-verify.description: Archive-then-clear-then-verify when reusing a pre-assigned dashboard with prior data: copy to Archive/{date}_{task_name}/, rm -f progress/*.json, reset logs.json, verify ls is empty before writing initialization.json.

status-derivation-algorithm-read-agents.description: Status derivation algorithm — read agents[] from initialization.json, build {taskId: progressData} map from progress/, fold per-agent statuses into overall status using fixed condition table.

two-source-merge-pattern-initialization.description: Two-source merge pattern: initialization.json provides {id, title, wave, layer, directory, depends_on}; progress/{id}.json provides {status, started_at, completed_at, summary, stage, message, milestones, deviations, logs}.

wave-status-derived-per-wave.description: Wave status is derived per-wave: completed if ALL agents terminal, in_progress if ANY active, pending otherwise.

five-fixed-phases-reading-context.description: Five fixed phases: reading_context (Diagnose) → planning (Plan the Fix) → implementing → testing (Verify) → finalizing/completed.

three-fix-categories-simple-fix.description: Three fix categories: Simple fix (typo/path) → continue original approach; Approach adjustment → log MODERATE deviation, implement; Major deviation → STOP and escalate.

diagnosis-log-format-diagnosis-root.description: Diagnosis log format: `Diagnosis: {root cause}. Previous worker failed at stage '{stage}' because {reason}.`

plan-log-format-plan-what.description: Plan log format: `Plan: {what you will do differently}. Keeping {X} from previous attempt, reverting {Y}.`

repair-task-progress-file-conventions.description: Repair-task progress-file conventions: summary `REPAIR: ...`, deviations prefixed `REPAIR DEVIATION:`, mandatory diagnosis milestone.

hub-modules-layout-file-gives.description: Hub-and-modules layout: this file gives constraints + summary + module index, agent/master/*.md contain detailed procedures.

five-master-responsibilities-gather-cont.description: Five master responsibilities: Gather Context, Plan, Dispatch, Status, Report — nothing else.

convention-map-per-task-filtered.description: Convention map → per-task filtered injection — master extracts CLAUDE.md categories from {project_root}, includes only relevant ones in each worker prompt.

worker-prompt-budget-target-8000.description: Worker-prompt budget target ~8000 tokens (~800 lines), task duration target 1-5 minutes.

master-writes-exactly-four-dashboard.description: Master writes exactly four dashboard files: initialization.json (write-once static plan), logs.json (append-only event log), master_state.json (state checkpoint after every event), metrics.json (once at swarm end).

all-dashboard-writes-atomic-read.description: All dashboard writes are atomic — read full file, modify in memory, write full file.

pki-integration-as-enhancement-not.description: PKI integration as enhancement-not-requirement — if no manifest.json exists, master proceeds with standard planning.

three-tier-hierarchy-meta-planner.description: Three-tier hierarchy: meta-planner (multi-stream) → child master (one stream/dashboard) → workers (one task each).

identical-schema-between-dashboards-id.description: Identical schema between dashboards/{id}/ and queue/{queueId}/ — promotion is a file copy plus log entry plus registry update plus queue clear.

stream-registry-shape-stream-id.description: Stream registry shape: {stream_id, slug, slot_type, slot_id, status, depends_on (cross-stream), child_master_agent_id, total_tasks, completed_tasks, failed_tasks}.

slot-assignment-priority-available-dashb.description: Slot assignment priority: available dashboards first (in listDashboards order), then queue slots queue1/queue2/... for overflow.

promotion-priority-when-multiple-eligibl.description: Promotion priority when multiple eligible: cross-stream-deps-satisfied first, then lower queue number, then more downstream dependents.

child-master-prompt-fully-self.description: Child-master prompt is fully self-contained: dashboard ID + required reading + stream scope + affected directories + conventions (extracted from CLAUDE.md by meta-planner) + reference code + cross-stream context + job sequence + execution rules + return format.

every-child-master-return-parse.description: On every child-master return: parse → update registry → one-line terminal confirmation → cache for downstream → promotion scan → check entire queue for newly-unblocked streams (a single completion may satisfy deps for non-first-in-line queue items).

fixed-stage-sequence-reading-context.description: Fixed stage sequence: reading_context → planning → implementing → testing → finalizing → completed | failed.

atomic-full-file-writes-worker.description: Atomic full-file writes — worker is sole owner of {task_id}.json, no read-modify-write cycle needed.

three-severity-deviation-classification-.description: Three-severity deviation classification: CRITICAL (changes downstream contracts, may trigger replanning), MODERATE (different approach, same outcome), MINOR (cosmetic).

upstream-dependency-4-step-procedure.description: Upstream dependency 4-step procedure: read upstream progress files → extract critical info → adapt approach → log findings.

pki-retrieval-6-step-flow.description: PKI retrieval 6-step flow: check manifest.json → identify task files → look up via files object + domain_index/tag_index → read 3-5 annotation files → check insights_index → log what was consumed.

annotations-schema-per-relative-path.description: Annotations schema: per relative-path key with optional gotchas/patterns/conventions string arrays.

structured-return-status-summary-files.description: Structured return: STATUS / SUMMARY / FILES CHANGED / EXPORTS / DIVERGENT ACTIONS — quantified one-line summaries.

hub-modules-layout-file-index.description: Hub-and-modules layout: this file is the index/quick-start, agent/worker/*.md hold detailed procedures.

compressed-schema-documentation-single-j.description: Compressed schema documentation — single JSON example with field-name | type | description table.

five-mandatory-writes-vs-full.description: Five mandatory writes (vs FULL's eight): before starting work, on every stage transition, on any deviation, on completion, on failure.

same-fixed-stages-as-full.description: Same fixed stages as FULL: reading_context → planning → implementing → testing → finalizing → completed | failed.

same-return-format-as-full.description: Same return format as FULL: STATUS / SUMMARY / FILES CHANGED / EXPORTS / DIVERGENT ACTIONS / WARNINGS / ERRORS — with omit-if-none rule for optional sections.

same-annotations-schema-as-full.description: Same annotations schema as FULL but emphasized as 'Optional'.

verification-needed-decision-table-modif.description: Verification-needed decision table: Modified existing across multiple files = dispatch verification; purely additive = optional; deviations reported = strongly recommended; all clean = may skip.

verification-agent-prompt-template-inclu.description: Verification agent prompt template includes Files Changed list, what-to-verify checklist (tests, type-check, build, integration), and structured return (TESTS/TYPES/BUILD/ISSUES).

metric-computation-elapsed-max-completed.description: Metric computation: elapsed = max(completed_at) - min(started_at); serial_estimate = sum of all task durations; parallel_efficiency = serial / elapsed (>1.0 = speedup); max_concurrent = peak overlap of started_at/completed_at windows.

final-report-quality-bar-developer.description: Final report quality bar: a developer not present during the swarm should fully understand (1) what was done, (2) what went sideways, (3) current project state, (4) next steps.

knowledge-extraction-insight-categories-.description: Knowledge-extraction insight categories: dependency_insights, complexity_surprises, failure_patterns, effective_patterns, architecture_notes.

post-swarm-transition-checklist-5.description: Post-Swarm Transition Checklist (5 conditions): all tasks terminal + final report delivered + metrics recorded + dashboard archived (if requested) + user-acknowledgment.

worker-prompt-template-version-p.description: Worker prompt TEMPLATE_VERSION: p_track_v2 — workers MUST include this in their progress file's template_version field on every write.

worker-prompt-sections-description-conte.description: Worker prompt sections: Description / Context / Project+Tracker Roots / Conventions (filtered) / Reference Code / Upstream Results (only for downstream tasks) / Sibling Tasks (optional, for same-wave file-area awareness) / Critical / Success Criteria (specific verifiable conditions) / Files (READ/MODIFY/CREATE actions) / Directory.

preparation-required-block-three-step.description: Preparation Required block — three-step pre-work flow: read plan.json + your task entry, read CLAUDE.md if not extracted, run readiness checklist (file paths listed, pattern reference read, one-sentence task statement, files exist at expected paths, upstream exports confirmed); after 3 fruitless reads, report as blocker.

eager-dispatch-loop-every-completion.description: Eager dispatch loop — on EVERY completion: parse return → validate → update task file → append logs → cache result → scan ALL pending tasks across ALL waves → dispatch every newly unblocked task IMMEDIATELY → circuit breaker check.

upstream-result-summary-format-per.description: Upstream Result Summary Format per dependency: STATUS / SUMMARY / FILES CHANGED / NEW EXPORTS / DEVIATIONS / KEY DETAILS — list each in its own `--- Dependency ---` block, ordered by relevance.

master-state-checkpoint-schema-completed.description: Master State Checkpoint schema: completed[], in_progress[], failed[] with repair_id, ready_to_dispatch[], upstream_results{} (one-line summaries per task), next_agent_number counter, permanently_failed[].

compaction-recovery-detect-missing-cache.description: Compaction Recovery: detect via missing cache for completed task → re-read all completed progress files → rebuild cache from task_id+summary+milestones+deviations → log warn → resume dispatch with rebuilt cache.

terminal-output-minimal-dispatch-one.description: Terminal output minimal: dispatch one-liner, completion one-liner, failure one-liner, deviation one-liner — full status tables only on !status.

11-step-planning-flow-resolve.description: 11-step planning flow: resolve roots → read instructions → parse → deep analysis (with dep_graph) → read context (with convention_map) → decompose (with budget+relevance checks) → choose Waves/Chains → write plan.json (hook-validated) → verify deps → check tracking thresholds → select+populate dashboard → present plan → approval gate.

plan-json-schema-name-created.description: plan.json schema: {name, created, context: {prompt, project_root, tracker_root, dashboard_id, type, directories, conventions: convention_map, reference_code[], architectural_decisions, edge_cases, shared_constraints}, tasks[]: {id, title, wave, depends_on, directory, description, approach, files[], context, critical, success_criteria[], instruction_mode, tags[]}}.

convention-map-categories-naming-file.description: convention_map categories: naming, file_structure, imports, frontend_styling, backend_api, error_handling, testing, types — held in working memory, used to filter per-worker.

convention-relevance-checklist-step-6d.description: Convention Relevance Checklist (Step 6D) — Include When/Skip When per-category cross-referenced against each task's files+description.

dependency-validation-algorithm-topologi.description: Dependency validation algorithm: topological sort + depth computation + bottleneck detection + orphan check.

approval-gate-4-step-protocol.description: Approval Gate 4-step protocol: write `permission` log entry → present explicit prompt → HALT → on approval log info entry and activate eager dispatch.

dashboard-claiming-follows-pre-assigned.description: Dashboard claiming follows pre-assigned-binding-is-absolute rule (no scanning, no auto-selection).

atomic-full-file-write-master.description: Atomic full-file write for master_state.json after every dispatch/completion/failure event (master is sole writer, no read-modify-write needed).

6-step-compaction-recovery-re.description: 6-step compaction recovery: re-read role -> read master_state.json -> read initialization.json -> read all progress files -> cross-reference and rebuild upstream cache -> resume eager dispatch.

metrics-json-computation-parallel-effici.description: metrics.json computation: parallel_efficiency = serial_estimate_seconds / elapsed_seconds; elapsed_seconds = max(completed_at) - min(started_at); max_concurrent computed from overlapping started_at/completed_at windows.

compaction-detection-log-warn-entry.description: On compaction detection, log a 'warn' entry to logs.json: 'Context compaction detected — rebuilt upstream cache from {N} progress files. File change data may be incomplete.'

p-track-writes-5-files.description: !p_track writes 5 files: initialization.json (write-once), logs.json (continuous), progress/{id}.json (workers), master_state.json (after every event), metrics.json (write-once at end).

p-writes-2-files-initialization.description: !p writes 2 files: initialization.json (write-once) + logs.json (init entry + final batch). NO master_state.json or metrics.json.

auto-escalation-if-3-parallel.description: Auto-escalation: if 3+ parallel agents OR >1 wave -> full !p_track tracking; otherwise lightweight.

both-modes-follow-same-dashboard.description: Both modes follow the same dashboard-selection priority chain: assigned dashboard (NON-NEGOTIABLE) -> --dashboard flag -> ask user.

master-written-progress-file-schema.description: Master-written progress file schema in lightweight mode: status, started_at, completed_at, summary, assigned_agent, stage, message, milestones[], deviations[], logs[], files_changed[].

parse-worker-files-changed-return.description: Parse worker FILES CHANGED return into files_changed[]: {created|modified|deleted} prefix becomes the action field.

master-writes-exactly-four-files.description: Master writes exactly four files during a swarm: initialization.json, logs.json, master_state.json, metrics.json. Plus plan.json which must exist FIRST (hook-enforced via validate-plan-required.sh).

logs-json-append-only-read.description: logs.json append-only: read full file -> parse -> append entry -> stringify -> write full file.

repair-task-append-new-agents.description: Repair task append: new agents[] entry with id '{wave}.{next}r', title 'REPAIR: {original}', same wave/layer/directory, identical depends_on; increment task.total_tasks and waves[].total; rewire all references in other agents' depends_on arrays.

archive-before-clear-read-initialization.description: Archive-before-clear: read initialization.json -> if task!=null copy entire dashboard dir to Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/ -> then delete progress files and reset initialization.json/logs.json.

dashboard-merges-initialization-json-sta.description: Dashboard merges initialization.json (static) with progress/{task_id}.json files (dynamic) client-side — all stat counts derive from progress files.

5-step-dispatch-build-completed.description: 5-step dispatch: build completed set from progress files (status==='completed') -> build in_progress set -> iterate agents[] checking (id not completed) AND (id not in_progress) AND (every depends_on entry IS in completed set) -> dispatch ALL passing tasks in parallel -> log each dispatch.

server-s-automatic-dependency-tracking.description: Server's automatic dependency tracking: fs.watch detects completion -> 100ms settle delay -> DependencyService.computeNewlyUnblocked() -> broadcasts tasks_unblocked SSE event with {dashboardId, completedTaskId, unblocked[]}.

get-api-dashboards-id-dispatchable.description: GET /api/dashboards/:id/dispatchable returns all tasks with every dependency completed and no progress file (still pending).

repair-task-dispatch-flow-log.description: Repair task dispatch flow: log failure -> create repair agent in initialization.json -> rewire depends_on -> dispatch repair worker with failed_task.md protocol -> run eager dispatch scan as normal.

7-step-single-task-failure.description: 7-step single-task failure recovery: (0) check double-failure -> (1) log error -> (2) append repair task to initialization.json -> (3) rewire depends_on chains -> (4) update chains[] if applicable -> (5) dispatch repair worker with failed_task.md protocol -> (6) log dispatch -> (7) run eager dispatch scan.

circuit-breaker-7-step-replanning.description: Circuit breaker 7-step replanning: pause dispatches -> gather failure context (read all progress files) -> analyze root cause -> produce revision plan with 4 categories (modified, added, removed, retry) -> apply revision to initialization.json -> log replanning outcome -> resume dispatch.

revision-categories-modified-update-exis.description: Revision categories: modified (update existing pending tasks), added (new repair tasks with 'r'-suffixed IDs), removed (no longer viable, clean from depends_on arrays), retry (delete progress files for re-dispatch as-is).

worker-return-validation-order-parse.description: Worker return validation order: parse -> validate STATUS -> validate SUMMARY -> validate FILES CHANGED -> process DIVERGENT ACTIONS -> handle PARTIAL -> proceed to eager dispatch.

13-item-pre-write-checklist.description: 13-item pre-write checklist: kebab-case name -> 'Waves'|'Chains' type -> ISO 8601 created -> total_tasks==agents.length -> total_waves==waves.length -> agents.length>=1 AND waves.length>=1 -> id regex match -> id uniqueness -> agent.wave references waves[j].id -> depends_on references existing IDs -> sum(waves.total)==agents.length -> Chains rules -> correct dashboard path.

atomic-full-file-write-read.description: Atomic full-file write: read -> parse -> modify -> stringify (2-space indent) -> write. Partial writes silently freeze dashboard.

repair-task-ids-r-suffix.description: Repair task IDs use 'r' suffix and don't break the regex: '2.1r' is valid.

6-step-pre-planning-flow.description: 6-step pre-planning flow: (1) check manifest exists -> (2) extract domains/tags/concepts from prompt -> (3) look up files via reverse indexes -> (4) read annotations for matched files (cap 8-10) -> (5) build PKI knowledge block -> (6) inject into worker prompts and inform decomposition.

extraction-priority-per-annotation-gotch.description: Extraction priority per annotation: gotchas (critical) -> patterns -> conventions -> exports -> imports_from -> relationships -> purpose.

knowledge-block-injection-point-appended.description: Knowledge block injection point: appended to CONVENTIONS section after CLAUDE.md-derived conventions, under [PKI] label.

ranking-signals-complexity-high-prioriti.description: Ranking signals: complexity:'high' (prioritize), stale:true (deprioritize), domains array length, appears in multiple indexes.

decomposition-signals-complexity-high-fi.description: Decomposition signals: complexity:'high' files get dedicated tasks, 3+ gotchas need narrow scope, serves/consumes relationships imply same wave or explicit dependency, stale files need fresh reads in READ list.

five-responsibilities-1-gather-context.description: Five responsibilities: (1) gather context — read CLAUDE.md, project CLAUDE.md, TOC, additional context dirs, source files; (2) plan — decompose, map dependencies, write self-contained worker prompts including {tracker_root} and {project_root} paths; (3) dispatch — Task tool with complete prompts, parallel for independent tasks, immediate scan on completions; (4) status — append to logs.json, do not update initialization.json; (5) report — final summary, update TOC if files moved, move to history.

master-conductor-metaphor-conductor-does.description: Master conductor metaphor: 'A conductor does not pick up a violin mid-symphony.' The master's power is its elevated perspective — losing it to implementation tunnel-vision causes dispatch errors and conflicts.

every-worker-prompt-must-include.description: Every worker prompt MUST include {tracker_root} (for progress reporting) AND {project_root} (for code work) — workers cannot auto-detect these.

two-layout-types-waves-vertical.description: Two layout types: 'Waves' (vertical columns, horizontal scroll, BFS-pathfinding dependency lines) for broad shallow work; 'Chains' (horizontal rows flowing left-to-right with SVG lines that turn green on completion) for narrow deep pipelines.

agent-card-3-row-anatomy.description: Agent card 3-row anatomy: top row (id badge + status dot + title), meta row (layer badge + directory badge + assigned_agent), bottom row varies by status (pending: 'Waiting...'; in_progress: stage badge + elapsed + milestone; completed: summary + duration; failed: red summary).

status-left-border-color-completed.description: Status -> left border color: completed=green #34d399, in_progress=purple #9b7cf0 (animated pulse), failed=red #ef4444, pending=gray, claimed=lime.

log-panel-hover-filter-filter.description: Log panel hover/filter: filter buttons for All/Info/Warn/Error/Deviation, auto-scroll unless user manually scrolled up.

card-hover-highlights-incoming-dependenc.description: Card hover highlights: incoming dependencies blue, outgoing (blocks) red, unrelated lines dim.

full-template-structure-template-version.description: Full template structure: TEMPLATE_VERSION header -> TASK header -> DESCRIPTION -> CONTEXT (with optional [PKI] relationships) -> PROJECT_ROOT/TRACKER_ROOT/PLAN FILE -> CONVENTIONS (filtered + optional [PKI] file knowledge) -> REFERENCE CODE -> UPSTREAM RESULTS -> SIBLING TASKS -> CRITICAL -> SUCCESS CRITERIA -> FILES (READ/MODIFY/CREATE) -> DIRECTORY -> PREPARATION steps -> LIVE PROGRESS REPORTING (FULL or LITE) -> DEVIATION REPORTING -> EXECUTION RULES -> RETURN FORMAT.

per-task-budget-conventions-200.description: Per-task budget: CONVENTIONS ~200 lines (PKI shares this; cap at 5 files), REFERENCE CODE ~100 lines, UPSTREAM RESULTS ~50 lines per dep, CONTEXT ~150 lines, total ~800 lines / ~8000 tokens.

pki-lookup-procedure-check-manifest.description: PKI lookup procedure: check manifest exists -> match task files against manifest.files -> filter out stale -> read annotations via hash -> prioritize MODIFY > READ files (cap 5) -> inject under [PKI] label in CONVENTIONS (gotchas/patterns/conventions) and CONTEXT (relationships).

pki-priority-order-when-budget.description: PKI priority order when budget tight: keep gotchas (1) -> patterns (2) -> relationships (3) -> conventions (4 — first to cut).

right-sizing-principle-each-task.description: Right-sizing principle: each task takes single agent 1-5 minutes; reads 2-3 files / modifies 1-2 files is typical. >10 reads or >5 modifies means decompose further.

upstream-results-injection-per-dependenc.description: UPSTREAM RESULTS injection per dependency: STATUS / SUMMARY (verbatim) / FILES CHANGED (with action) / NEW EXPORTS / DEVIATIONS / KEY DETAILS (1-2 sentences of specific technical context).

return-format-status-completed-failed.description: RETURN FORMAT: STATUS (completed|failed) / SUMMARY / FILES CHANGED (with action) / EXPORTS (omit if none) / DIVERGENT ACTIONS (omit if none) / WARNINGS (omit if none) / ERRORS (omit if none).

workers-also-produce-pki-annotations.description: Workers also PRODUCE PKI annotations via ANNOTATIONS section in return + annotations field in progress file — master merges these into PKI for next swarm (feedback loop).

severity-tagged-deviations-every-deviati.description: Severity-tagged deviations: every deviation entry MUST include a severity field (CRITICAL/MODERATE/MINOR), not optional.

dual-write-protocol-deviations-entry.description: Dual-write protocol: deviations[] entry + logs[] entry at level 'deviation' written together atomically.

ambiguity-resolution-choose-most-conserv.description: Ambiguity resolution: choose most conservative option (changes least, breaks nothing, follows existing patterns), document as MODERATE deviation, log at warn level.

severity-decision-rule-any-change.description: Severity decision rule: any change affecting an interface/API/contract downstream tasks depend on => CRITICAL; same outcome via different means => MODERATE; cosmetic/naming with no functional impact => MINOR.

full-file-atomic-overwrite-write.description: Full-file atomic overwrite via the Write tool (which uses tmp-rename internally) — the only correct way to update progress files.

fixed-seven-stage-lifecycle-progressed.description: Fixed seven-stage lifecycle progressed in order: reading_context → planning → implementing → testing → finalizing → completed/failed.

eight-mandatory-write-points-initial.description: Eight mandatory write points: initial write before work, after reading upstream deps, on every stage transition, on every deviation, on every error, on every file change, on completion, on failure.

append-only-semantics-milestones-deviati.description: Append-only semantics for milestones[], deviations[], logs[], files_changed[] (no removal once added).

annotations-sub-schema-gotchas-patterns.description: Annotations sub-schema (gotchas/patterns/conventions per file path) intentionally mirrors PKI per-file annotation structure for clean merging.

log-levels-follow-strict-color.description: Log levels follow strict color mapping: info=purple, warn=lime/yellow, error=red, deviation=yellow.

plain-text-key-value-sections.description: Plain-text key-value sections (not JSON) with colon separators — designed for grep/eyeballing in terminal output.

conditional-sections-omit-entirely-if.description: Conditional sections: omit ENTIRELY if empty (no 'EXPORTS: (none)'). EXPORTS, ANNOTATIONS, WARNINGS, ERRORS, DIVERGENT ACTIONS all follow this rule.

files-changed-action-prefix-per.description: FILES CHANGED uses action prefix per line (created, modified, deleted) and parallels progress file files_changed[].

export-entry-format-type-function.description: Export entry format: '- {type: function|type|interface|endpoint|constant|file} {name} — {brief description}' — type tag is a 6-value enum.

annotation-entry-format-indented-file.description: Annotation entry format: indented file path followed by gotchas/patterns/conventions sub-lines (any combination, all optional).

shared-context-shape-exports-string.description: shared_context shape: { exports: string[], interfaces: string[], patterns: string[], notes: string } — populated as early as possible in the implementing stage so siblings can read it.

read-only-sibling-access-tracker.description: Read-only sibling access via {tracker_root}/dashboards/{dashboardId}/progress/{sibling_task_id}.json — same path schema as own progress file.

sibling-read-tracking-every-sibling.description: Sibling read tracking: every sibling read appended to sibling_reads[] (string array of task IDs) AND logged at info level even if the read returned no useful data.

case-shape-sibling-defines-interface.description: Use case shape: sibling A defines an interface in shared_context.interfaces; sibling B reads it to type its own implementation, avoiding redundant interface definition.

four-step-protocol-parallel-read.description: Four-step protocol: parallel read → extract critical info → adapt approach → log what you learned (with deviation entries for adaptations).

field-extraction-priority-status-gate.description: Field-extraction priority: status (gate), summary (one-line truth), deviations[] with focus on CRITICAL severity, milestones[] for what was actually built, logs[] scanned for error/warn, message for final state.

adaptation-logging-convention-log-at.description: Adaptation logging convention: log at 'info' level summarizing all upstream reads in one entry, then log each required adaptation as a separate 'deviation' level entry.

four-tier-resolution-hierarchy-explicit.description: Four-tier resolution hierarchy with explicit priority order (Synapse > project > user > project_root).

spec-based-command-design-each.description: Spec-based command design: each .md file is a complete spec the agent follows verbatim, not a hint or guideline.

categorized-command-catalog-7-categories.description: Categorized command catalog (7 categories) for user-facing discovery via !commands and !help.

mandatory-duplicate-detection-before-cre.description: Mandatory duplicate detection before creation, with overwrite/rename/cancel choice presented to user.

dashboard-merge-initialization-json-plan.description: Dashboard merge: initialization.json (plan) + progress/{id}.json (lifecycle) merged client-side to render agent cards.

dashboard-selection-priority-chat-spawne.description: Dashboard selection priority: chat-spawned directive > explicit --dashboard flag > ask user (never auto-scan/auto-select).

dependency-lines-drawn-bfs-pathfinding.description: Dependency lines drawn via BFS pathfinding through corridor gaps; lines never cross cards or title headers.

hover-interaction-model-hover-line.description: Hover interaction model: hover line → blue glow; hover card → its needs blue, things it blocks red, unrelated lines dim.

live-update-flow-worker-writes.description: Live update flow: worker writes progress file → server fs.watch detects → SSE 'agent_progress' broadcast → React re-render.

per-dashboard-file-layout-dashboards.description: Per-dashboard file layout: dashboards/{id}/{initialization,logs,master_state,metrics}.json + progress/{task_id}.json — replaces legacy single-root status.json.

sole-writer-atomic-overwrite-progress.description: Sole-writer atomic-overwrite for progress files: each worker owns exactly one file, writes the full JSON every update.

fs-watch-progress-fs-watchfile.description: fs.watch on progress/ + fs.watchFile on init/logs — different mechanisms for directory vs file watching.

stat-derivation-from-progress-files.description: Stat derivation from progress files: dashboard counts files by status field rather than maintaining counters.

sub-schemas-deviations-has-at.description: Sub-schemas: deviations[] has at/severity/description; logs[] has at/level/msg; milestones[] has at/msg; files_changed[] has path/action; annotations is keyed by relative file path.

dependency-driven-dispatch-not-wave.description: Dependency-driven dispatch (not wave-driven): scan ALL pending tasks for newly-satisfied dependencies on every completion event, dispatch every unblocked task immediately.

circuit-breaker-inline-replan-loop.description: Circuit breaker → inline replan loop: pause dispatches → read all progress files → analyze root cause → emit modified/added/removed/retry revision → apply to initialization.json → resume.

statusing-split-master-owns-event.description: Statusing split: master owns event log + task file updates; workers own full lifecycle + progress + detailed logs.

shared-file-pattern-preference-c.description: Shared file pattern preference: C (no shared file) > B (integration task) > A (owner task).
