lazy-require-inside-lifecycle-handlers.description: Lazy require() inside lifecycle handlers (require('./settings'), require('./ipc-handlers')) so module load order doesn't trigger early instantiation

debounced-window-state-persistence-500ms.description: Debounced window state persistence (500ms) — settings writes are I/O and would otherwise hammer disk during drag-resize

protocol-fallback-chain-direct-path.description: Protocol fallback chain: direct path -> dist/ -> public/ — accommodates both Vite-built assets and legacy public/ files

forwarding-broadcast-wrapper-autoupdates.description: Forwarding broadcast wrapper for AutoUpdateService: every emit checks win.isDestroyed() and webContents.isDestroyed() before send to avoid 'Object has been destroyed' errors during shutdown

broadcast-bridge-pattern-createbroadcast.description: Broadcast bridge pattern: createBroadcastFn returns a closure that mirrors the SSE broadcast signature so WatcherService is reused unchanged — only the transport differs (webContents.send vs SSE)

service-init-broadcastfn-injection-claud.description: Service init via broadcastFn injection: ClaudeCodeService.init(broadcastFn), CodexService.init(broadcastFn), etc. — services emit progress through this single channel

persisted-ordering-reconciliation-getord.description: Persisted ordering reconciliation: getOrderedDashboards filters meta.order against existing IDs, appends new ones by creation time, ensures 'ide' first, then writes back the reconciled order — self-healing across filesystem changes

dashboard-status-derivation-mirrors-src.description: Dashboard status derivation mirrors src/server/routes/apiRoutes.js (deriveDashboardStatus duplicated) — keeping IPC and HTTP responses identical when both modes are used

reverse-sorted-match-application-ide.description: Reverse-sorted match application in ide-search-replace (sort by line desc, column desc) so earlier replacements don't shift positions of later ones

chat-agent-allocation-4-hex.description: Chat agent allocation: 4-hex random with up to 1000 retries, uniqueness scoped across ALL chat dirs (not per-chat), folder layout Chat/chat{N}/agent{XXXX}/

git-operations-execfile-array-args.description: git operations use execFile + array args (never shell concat) for injection safety; all wrapped in gitExec() with consistent { success, data | error } envelope

diagnostics-multi-language-dispatch-ided.description: Diagnostics multi-language dispatch via ideDiagLanguage(filePath) -> {ideDiagJSON, ideDiagJS, ideDiagCSS}

initial-data-burst-pattern-sendinitialda.description: Initial-data burst pattern (sendInitialData): on window-ready, push dashboards_list + initialization + all_progress + queue_changed for every dashboard — mirrors what an SSE client would receive on /events connect

whitelist-gated-subscription-contextbrid.description: Whitelist-gated subscription: contextBridge exposes 'on' but channel names are validated against PUSH_CHANNELS before registering the listener

unsubscribe-return-every-successful-retu.description: Unsubscribe-by-return: every successful 'on' returns an unsubscribe function — idiomatic for React useEffect cleanup

1-1-method-channel-mapping.description: 1:1 method-to-channel mapping organized by domain banner comments

optional-args-forwarded-as-handler.description: Optional-args forwarded as-is to the handler so backend can apply defaults

event-payload-normalization-callback-rec.description: Event payload normalization: 'on' callback receives only the data argument (event stripped) — keeps renderer code free of Electron internals

lazy-file-location-resolution-electronap.description: Lazy file location resolution via electronApp.getPath('userData') in init() — the path is platform-specific and can only be obtained after app.whenReady

single-file-json-persistence-no.description: Single-file JSON persistence (no SQLite, no electron-store dependency) — keeps Synapse dependency-free for settings

default-fallback-get-returns-cache.description: Default-fallback get(): returns cache value if present, else DEFAULTS, else undefined — guarantees no thrown errors for missing keys

eager-auto-create-parent-directory.description: Eager auto-create of parent directory in _save() via fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })

module-level-singleton-state-object.description: Module-level singleton state object shared by every event handler; mutation through `setState(patch)` triggers an `emit()` that broadcasts the entire snapshot to the renderer.

renderer-push-channel-update-status.description: Renderer push channel is `update-status`; the snapshot is the source of truth (no incremental diffs).

lifecycle-initautoupdater-broadcast-at-a.description: Lifecycle: initAutoUpdater(broadcast) at app ready -> auto event-driven updates -> disposeAutoUpdater() on quit.

pid-keyed-activeworkers-map-single.description: PID-keyed activeWorkers map is the single source of truth; every callback (stdout/stderr/close/error) checks-and-mutates this map.

all-renderer-notifications-pushed-over.description: All renderer notifications are pushed over three named channels: `worker-output`, `worker-complete`, `worker-error` — kept stable so ipc-handlers/preload only needs to expose those three.

stream-json-ndjson-parsing-each.description: stream-json NDJSON parsing: each line is JSON.parse'd inside a try/catch and forwarded as `{chunk, parsed}` so the renderer can use either the raw text or structured event.

killworker-double-tap-sigterm-then.description: killWorker double-tap: SIGTERM, then a 5s SIGKILL fallback to guarantee dead workers.

mirrors-claudecodeservice-s-pid-keyed.description: Mirrors ClaudeCodeService's PID-keyed activeWorkers + 3-channel broadcast pattern (`worker-output`, `worker-complete`, `worker-error`) so callers can swap providers transparently.

lastmessage-roundtrip-tmpfile-because-co.description: lastMessage roundtrip via tmpfile because Codex doesn't surface the final assistant message in NDJSON the way Claude does.

provider-field-every-event-payload.description: Provider field on every event payload (`provider: 'codex'`) lets the renderer distinguish events when both providers run side-by-side.

two-tier-directory-model-built.description: Two-tier directory model: built-in `_commands/<folder>/*.md` (listCommands) vs user-authored `_commands/user/<folder>/*.md` (listUserCommands).

all-file-writing-goes-through.description: All file-writing goes through `safeName` sanitization to prevent path traversal in commandName.

markdown-introspection-simple-regex-h1.description: Markdown introspection by simple regex on H1 + **Purpose:** + **Syntax:** lines — no full markdown parser dependency.

ai-generation-reads-up-3.description: AI generation reads up to 3 example command files from the same target folder (truncated to 2000 chars each) for style transfer.

one-json-file-per-conversation.description: One JSON file per conversation in CONVERSATIONS_DIR (no index file). listConversations re-reads every file on every call.

sort-newest-first-updated-iso.description: Sort newest-first by `updated` (ISO string compared via Date.parse).

optional-dashboardid-surface-filtering-h.description: Optional dashboardId+surface filtering happens server-side rather than client-side.

stderr-scrape-pattern-parse-debugger.description: Stderr-scrape pattern: parse `Debugger listening on (ws://...)` regex from cumulative stderr buffer to find the CDP endpoint.

promise-bridged-cdp-commands-every.description: Promise-bridged CDP commands: every sendCDPCommand attaches a one-shot `message` listener that filters by `id`, with timeout cleanup.

renderer-push-events-4-channels.description: Renderer push events use 4 channels: `debug-paused`, `debug-resumed`, `debug-output`, `debug-stopped`. The paused payload includes callStack, scopes, pausedFile, pausedLine, hitBreakpoints — enough for a full IDE pause UI in one frame.

two-evaluate-paths-debugger-evaluateonca.description: Two evaluate paths: Debugger.evaluateOnCallFrame (paused state, with frame context) vs Runtime.evaluate (global runtime); same return shape via an inline RemoteObject -> simplified-shape mapper.

two-pass-instrumentation-1-collect.description: Two-pass instrumentation: (1) collect all valid tag matches with their attribute slices, (2) rebuild output string by walking matches and inserting ` data-synapse-label="..."` right before the closing `>`.

hand-rolled-jsx-aware-tag.description: Hand-rolled JSX-aware tag closer (findTagClose) instead of using a JSX/babel parser — keeps the dependency surface tiny but means tricky edge cases (TS generics, escaped templates) are unsupported.

idempotent-existing-data-synapse-label.description: Idempotent: existing data-synapse-label attributes are detected and skipped via simple substring search of the attrs slice.

mtime-based-cache-invalidation-cachedmti.description: mtime-based cache invalidation: `cachedMtimes[file] !== fs.statSync(file).mtimeMs` triggers full rescan.

extracttextcontent-strips-jsx-expression.description: extractTextContent strips JSX expressions and child tags then collapses whitespace — surfaces only the literal text, matching what the user sees in the preview overlay.

path-relative-label-entries-path.description: Path-relative label entries (`path.relative(projectPath, filePath)`) so the renderer-side overlay can treat them as portable strings.

uuid-based-unambiguous-lookup-labels.description: UUID-based unambiguous lookup — labels are crypto.randomUUID() so a single matching file is sufficient (no collision handling needed).

three-tier-framework-detection-1.description: Three-tier framework detection: (1) config file presence, (2) package.json scripts substring match, (3) framework-prioritized port probe order.

surgical-text-replacement-distinguish-pu.description: Surgical text replacement: distinguish 'pure-text' content (replace whole inner) from 'mixed' content (segment-walk via non-text regex /<[^>]*>|\{[^}]*\}/ then replace first text segment).

tcp-probe-net-socket-timeout.description: TCP probe via net.Socket with timeout=500ms; first success wins.

language-detection-signature-file-lookup.description: Language detection via signature-file lookup table (DETECT_FILES).

scandirectory-builds-tree-shaped-node.description: scanDirectory builds a tree-shaped node array with directories first then alphabetical within each level — used for the file-tree display in the project setup modal.

multi-source-project-context-primary.description: Multi-source project context: primary CLAUDE.md + additional context dirs are merged into a single contexts[] array, later partitioned in SwarmOrchestrator.dispatchReady by path-prefix match.

three-stage-prompt-construction-system.description: Three-stage prompt construction (system / task / replan) all using a parts[] array + final parts.join('\n') idiom.

pki-auto-enrichment-runs-at.description: PKI auto-enrichment runs at prompt-build time — workers don't see knowledge unless their task title/description match an indexed domain/tag/concept.

hard-coded-budgets-top-8.description: Hard-coded budgets: top-8 relevant files, top-15 patterns, top-10 conventions, top-8 insights, last-5 swarm insights — keeps the PKI block under ~100 lines.

defensive-id-validation-buildsystempromp.description: Defensive ID validation: buildSystemPrompt throws on invalid dashboardId rather than letting the worker write into the wrong dashboard.

single-memory-state-map-activeswarms.description: Single in-memory state map `activeSwarms[dashboardId] = {state, projectPath, provider, model, cliPath, dispatchedTasks, completedTasks, failedTasks, ...}`.

reactive-dispatch-loop-every-ontaskcompl.description: Reactive dispatch loop: every onTaskComplete / onTaskFailed / resumeSwarm calls dispatchReady() which scans the agents[] for tasks with all deps satisfied and spawns workers for them.

provider-agnostic-worker-dispatch-claude.description: Provider-agnostic worker dispatch: ClaudeCodeService and CodexService share the spawnWorker(opts) signature, swapped at call site.

two-phase-replan-spawn-cli.description: Two-phase replan: spawn CLI -> parse JSON -> mutate initialization.json on disk -> resume. The CLI runs single-turn (--print, --output-format text, no streaming).

post-swarm-knowledge-extraction-deviatio.description: Post-swarm knowledge extraction: deviation severity drives insight category (CRITICAL -> dependency_insights, MODERATE -> architecture_notes, failed status -> failure_patterns).

appendlog-always-re-reads-logs.description: appendLog always re-reads logs.json (defensive against external writers) and pushes a fixed schema {timestamp, task_id, agent, level, message, task_name}.

read-modify-write-pattern-every.description: Read-modify-write pattern: every public function reads init, mutates it, calls writeInit().

falls-back-default-initialization-deep.description: Falls back to DEFAULT_INITIALIZATION (deep-cloned via JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(...))) if the dashboard has no init file yet.

recalcwaves-enforces-invariants-wave-tot.description: recalcWaves enforces invariants: wave.total reflects current agents, empty waves are pruned, init.task.total_tasks/total_waves are updated.

pty-based-terminal-sessions-streaming.description: PTY-based terminal sessions with streaming via the `terminal-output` push channel.

two-channel-push-protocol-terminal.description: Two-channel push protocol: `terminal-output` (per-chunk data) and `terminal-exit` (one-shot at process exit).

id-keyed-activeterminals-map-cleanup.description: ID-keyed activeTerminals map with cleanup-on-exit — onExit handler deletes the entry before broadcasting.

killterminal-calls-proc-kill-no.description: killTerminal calls proc.kill() with no signal arg — node-pty defaults to SIGHUP which closes the PTY cleanly.

doc-as-config-file-loaded.description: Doc-as-config: this file is loaded by the harness (NOT by application code) — the 'IMPORTANT: These instructions OVERRIDE any default behavior' lead is the trigger that elevates it to system-prompt status.

path-convention-pattern-every-operationa.description: Path convention pattern: every operational doc in the repo uses {tracker_root} / {project_root} placeholders rather than concrete paths to keep agent instructions portable across machines.

layered-protocol-pattern-claude-md.description: Layered protocol pattern: CLAUDE.md (short orientation) → AGENTS.md (full protocol) → agent/master/*.md + agent/worker/*.md (deep references) → .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md (loaded on demand).

mode-taxonomy-serial-parallel-tracked.description: Mode taxonomy: Serial / Parallel-tracked (`!p_track`) / Lightweight-parallel (`!p`) / Multi-stream (`!master_plan_track`) — every command in the repo maps onto one of these execution modes.

pattern-what-changes-synapse-before.description: Pattern: 'What changes with Synapse' before/after table appears immediately after the badges to set value framing in <100 words.

section-ordering-getting-started-usage.description: Section ordering: Getting Started → Usage Guide → Features → Commands → Best Practices → How It Works (decision-tree first, deep-dive last).

comparison-tables-p-track-vs.description: Comparison tables (`!p_track` vs `!p` vs `!master_plan_track`) appear at every decision point so users don't have to memorize differences.

every-prerequisite-paired-verification-c.description: Every prerequisite is paired with a verification command (`node --version`, `npm --version`).

pattern-non-negotiable-absolute-inviolab.description: Pattern: 'NON-NEGOTIABLE' / 'absolute, inviolable constraint' rhetorical framing for the master no-code rule — repeated three times within the same section to maximize agent compliance.

pattern-every-protocol-section-ends.description: Pattern: every protocol section ends with a positive list and a negative list (what to do / what NEVER to do).

pattern-resolution-order-lists-numbered.description: Pattern: 'Resolution order' lists are numbered 1..N and explicitly say 'Once found, read the command file in full and follow it exactly.' to discourage improvisation.

pattern-timestamp-protocol-date-u.description: Pattern: timestamp protocol uses `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"` everywhere — UTC ISO-8601 with seconds precision, no fractional seconds.

architecture-as-narrative-each-subsystem.description: Architecture-as-narrative: each subsystem has a section that opens with a one-paragraph statement of purpose, then bullets the concrete capabilities — designers can read it top-to-bottom without code knowledge.

pattern-every-claim-about-agent.description: Pattern: every claim about agent behavior is anchored to a concrete file (e.g., 'see `electron/services/SwarmOrchestrator.js`') so the doc remains auditable.

comparison-framing-conductor-does-not.description: Comparison framing: 'A conductor does not pick up a violin mid-symphony' / 'Air traffic controllers don't fly the planes' — recurring metaphors used to motivate the developer-in-the-loop philosophy.

pattern-build-chain-vite-build.description: Pattern: build chain — `vite build` → produces dist/ → Electron loads dist/index.html via app:// protocol (per electron/main.js).

pattern-dev-chain-concurrently-k.description: Pattern: dev chain — `concurrently -k "vite" "electron ."` runs Vite dev server (port 5174 per vite.config.js) AND Electron in parallel; -k kills both when one exits.

pattern-native-module-rebuild-electron.description: Pattern: native module rebuild — `electron-rebuild` pins node-pty's binary ABI to the installed Electron version every time deps change.

pattern-extraresources-files-separation-.description: Pattern: extraResources + files separation — files are bundled into the asar archive, extraResources are copied alongside (so dashboards/ stays writable on disk after install).

pattern-alias-renderer-only-directory.description: Pattern: '@' alias to the renderer-only directory keeps the boundary between Electron main and React renderer enforceable via import paths.

pattern-worker-pre-bundling-monaco.description: Pattern: worker pre-bundling for Monaco — the canonical Vite + Monaco recipe.

pattern-relative-base-electron-required.description: Pattern: relative `base` for Electron — required for any Vite app loaded from a non-HTTP origin.

standard-vite-react-entrypoint-empty.description: Standard Vite + React entrypoint: empty body, single mount node, single ES module script.

font-preconnect-pattern-preconnect-cross.description: Font preconnect pattern (preconnect + crossorigin) for performance — present even though the app is desktop and cold-start performance is dominated by Electron startup.

launch-sequence-pattern-cd-dirname.description: Launch sequence pattern: `cd $(dirname $0)` → ensure deps → exec dev/start command.

lazy-install-pattern-only-run.description: Lazy install pattern: only run `npm install` when `node_modules/` is missing — keeps subsequent launches fast.

port-as-singleton-pattern-known.description: Port-as-singleton pattern: use a known listening port to detect whether the app is already running, instead of a PID file.

fail-fast-pattern-refuse-start.description: Fail-fast pattern: refuse to start when conflict is detected, with a hint pointing the user at the matching `stop.sh`.

two-phase-shutdown-pattern-discover.description: Two-phase shutdown pattern: discover all candidate PIDs first, then act — minimizes race windows where a child respawns mid-script.

idempotent-shutdown-running-stop-sh.description: Idempotent shutdown: running stop.sh twice is harmless (second call exits with 'Synapse is not running').

self-relocating-launcher-pattern-cache.description: Self-relocating launcher pattern: cache repo path in a dotfile so the launcher script can be relocated freely.

profile-sourcing-pattern-explicitly-load.description: Profile-sourcing pattern: explicitly load `~/.zshrc` and `~/.bash_profile` because Finder's GUI launch context lacks them.

path-prepend-pattern-ensure-homebrew.description: PATH-prepend pattern: ensure Homebrew bin dirs are present even if the user's profile didn't add them.

production-launch-pattern-build-vite.description: Production-launch pattern: build via Vite, then exec Electron — same as `npm start`.

banner-pattern-prints-divider-title.description: Banner pattern: prints a divider/title before any action so the Terminal output is recognizable at a glance.

conflict-check-pattern-same-port.description: Conflict-check pattern: same port-busy check as start.sh, with the same 'use Stop Synapse.command' guidance.

same-two-phase-shutdown-pattern.description: Same two-phase shutdown pattern as stop.sh.

banner-keypress-close-pattern-optimized.description: Banner + keypress-to-close pattern: optimized for double-click UX where the Terminal window must stay open long enough for the user to read the result.

layered-ignore-pattern-granular-ignores.description: Layered ignore pattern: granular ignores at the top (specific files) plus broad wildcard ignores at the bottom (entire directories) — the wildcard wins, so the granular rules are de facto redundant in this repo.

runtime-vs-source-separation-every.description: Runtime-vs-source separation: every directory that holds runtime swarm artifacts (dashboards, tasks, Archive, history, queue) is excluded; everything else (electron/, src/, _commands/, agent/, documentation/) is tracked.

section-headers-comments-runtime-data.description: Section headers in comments (`# Runtime data`, `# Build output`, `# Dependencies`, `# OS`) document the ignore taxonomy.

single-source-truth-pattern-one.description: Single-source-of-truth pattern: one file at a known location, read by every command.

resolution-fallback-pattern-explicit-fla.description: Resolution-fallback pattern: explicit flag → this file → CWD (per CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md path-convention sections).

self-targeting-dogfooding-synapse-synaps.description: Self-targeting dogfooding: Synapse uses Synapse to build Synapse — proves the architecture works without special-casing.

pure-http-server-no-express.description: Pure-http server (no Express/Koa) — every endpoint is a manual `if (url.pathname === ... && req.method === ...)` branch.

api-routing-delegated-handleapiroute-req.description: API routing is delegated: `handleApiRoute(req, res, url)` returns boolean indicating whether the route was handled, and the SSE branch + 404 fallback are local.

per-client-dashboard-filtering-dashboard.description: Per-client dashboard filtering via `?dashboard=` query param stored in client metadata, used later by `broadcast` to skip non-matching clients.

connect-state-replay-send-dashboards.description: On-connect state replay: send dashboards_list -> per-dashboard initialization -> per-dashboard progress -> per-dashboard combined init_state -> queue_changed, so a fresh client renders without polling.

startup-directory-bootstrapping-dashboar.description: Startup directory bootstrapping (`dashboards/`, `Archive/`, `history/`, `queue/`) is idempotent — all use `fs.existsSync` + `mkdirSync({recursive:true})`.

graceful-shutdown-stops-watchers-before.description: Graceful shutdown stops watchers BEFORE heartbeat BEFORE SSE close — mirrors the reverse order of startup.

sse-heartbeat-every-15s-heartbeat.description: SSE heartbeat every 15s (HEARTBEAT_MS) to detect disconnects and prevent proxy timeouts.

per-client-metadata-dashboardfilter-stor.description: Per-client metadata (dashboardFilter) stored in the Map value, not the response object — keeps response untouched and enables future per-client options without prototype pollution.

lazy-disconnect-reaping-every-broadcast.description: Lazy disconnect reaping on every broadcast iteration — no separate sweep timer.

global-events-no-dashboardid-payload.description: Global events (no `dashboardId` in payload) bypass filter — `dashboards_list` and `queue_changed` reach all clients regardless of subscription.

cascade-dispatch-every-route-sequential.description: Cascade dispatch — every route is a sequential if/return-true; no router lib.

path-traversal-defense-sanitizepathparam.description: Path traversal defense via `sanitizePathParam`: rejects `..`, `/`, `\`, and anything outside `[a-zA-Z0-9_.\-]` (must start with alphanumeric or underscore), max 100 chars.

parsedashboardroute-pathname-regex-api-d.description: `parseDashboardRoute(pathname)` regex `^/api/dashboards/([^/]+)(?:/(.*))?$` captures id + optional subpath in one match, so all `/api/dashboards/:id/<subpath>` endpoints share the same validated id.

async-db-style-reads-fs.description: Async DB-style reads use `fs.promises` via IIFE; sync mutations use `fs.writeFileSync` for in-request atomicity.

sendjson-res-statuscode-data-canonical.description: `sendJSON(res, statusCode, data)` is the canonical JSON response helper — sets `Content-Type: application/json` and stringifies the payload.

default-fallbacks-default-initialization.description: Default fallbacks via `{ ...DEFAULT_INITIALIZATION }` and `{ ...DEFAULT_LOGS }` (spread to avoid shared-reference mutation) when reads return null.

all-paths-resolved-at-module.description: All paths resolved at module load via `path.resolve` / `path.join` — never relative.

timing-constants-suffixed-ms-millisecond.description: Timing constants suffixed with `_MS` (milliseconds), `_MS` for poll/debounce/retry distinguishes from raw counts.

default-shape-constants-default-minimal.description: Default-shape constants (`DEFAULT_*`) are minimal valid documents that pass schema validation in utils/json.js.

read-fail-silently-pattern-try.description: Read-fail-silently pattern: `try {return JSON.parse(readFileSync(p))} catch {return null}` — callers branch on null.

mid-write-resilience-readjsonwithretry-w.description: Mid-write resilience: `readJSONWithRetry` waits 80ms and retries once before giving up — designed for fs.watch races against worker writes.

atomic-write-write-path-tmp.description: Atomic write: write to `<path>.tmp`, rename to `<path>`. Best-effort cleanup of .tmp on failure.

schema-validation-returns-boolean-caller.description: Schema validation returns boolean — callers log/skip on false, never throw.

cross-worker-write-detection-expectedtas.description: Cross-worker write detection: `expectedTaskId` mismatch in progress data returns false AND logs to stderr — this is a hook-enforced security boundary.

kahn-s-algorithm-cycle-detection.description: Kahn's algorithm for cycle detection: build in-degree + adjacency, BFS from in-degree-0 nodes, cycle iff sorted.length < total.

four-phase-validation-self-ref.description: Four-phase validation: self_ref -> dangling_ref -> cycle -> orphan. Each phase appends to errors/warnings independently — all checks always run, no short-circuit.

errors-block-warnings-inform-only.description: Errors block, warnings inform: only errors flip `valid: false`.

wave-1-exemption-convention-root.description: Wave 1 exemption convention — root tasks have no upstream dependencies and may have no downstream dependents (e.g. terminal write tasks).

type-discrimination-type-field-each.description: Type discrimination via `type` field on each error/warning ('self_ref', 'dangling_ref', 'cycle', 'orphan') — UI uses this to color/group.

single-source-truth-archive-directory.description: Single source-of-truth for archive directory layout: ARCHIVE_DIR constant from utils/constants is the only place the path is defined.

defensive-try-catch-wrapping-listarchive.description: Defensive try/catch wrapping listArchives so missing/unreadable Archive dir returns [] rather than throwing into route handlers.

lazy-require-inside-function-body.description: Lazy require inside function body (`require('./DashboardService')`) used as a circular-dep-avoidance pattern.

sync-async-parallel-pairs-readdashboardi.description: Sync + Async parallel pairs (readDashboardInit / readDashboardInitAsync etc.) — sync used in tight loops (watcher reconciliation, listDashboards) where blocking I/O is acceptable; async used by IPC handler aggregations.

atomic-write-pattern-utils-json.description: Atomic write pattern via utils/json.writeAtomic — used for default file seeding to prevent half-written defaults if the process crashes during ensureDashboard.

validity-gate-pattern-listdashboards-fil.description: Validity-gate pattern: listDashboards() filters by `existsSync(initialization.json)` — used elsewhere as 'this dashboard is real'.

object-keyed-task-id-return.description: Object-keyed-by-task_id return shape for progress: callers (DependencyService, HistoryService, WatcherService) all assume `progressMap[task_id]` lookup.

incremental-vs-full-scan-pair.description: Incremental-vs-full scan pair: getDispatchableTasks (O(agents * deps)) for cold start, computeNewlyUnblocked (O(downstream_of_completed)) for hot path — invoked by WatcherService DEPENDENCY_CHECK_DELAY_MS after a completion event.

output-augmentation-returned-agent-objec.description: Output augmentation: returned agent objects are spread copies with an extra `dependency_status` field — original initialization.json data is not mutated.

helper-trio-getagents-getdependson-resol.description: Helper trio (getAgents, getDependsOn, resolveTaskStatus) defensively handle missing/non-array fields — defends against partial initialization.json writes.

pending-as-default-missing-progress.description: Pending-as-default for missing progress: resolveTaskStatus returns 'pending' for any task without a progress file — this is the correct semantics for unblock checks.

build-then-save-pattern-buildhistorysumm.description: Build-then-save pattern: buildHistorySummary is pure (no I/O writes), saveHistorySummary composes it with the write step — easy to test build logic in isolation.

aggregation-walk-single-pass-over.description: Aggregation walk: single pass over init.agents accumulates counts, summaries, and timing arrays simultaneously.

date-stamping-new-date-toisostring.description: Date stamping uses `new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)` (UTC) — same convention as ArchiveService, ensures dates align across both systems.

mirror-dashboardservice-api-surface-same.description: Mirror of DashboardService API surface — same function names with `Queue` prefix. Maintained intentionally so consumers can swap dashboards/queue accessors with minimal change.

three-state-derivation-pending-progress.description: Three-state derivation (pending/in_progress/completed/error) computed live from progress walk rather than stored — queue items have no separate status field, status is always derived.

validity-gate-initialization-json-exists.description: Validity gate via initialization.json existsSync — same pattern as DashboardService.listDashboards.

three-tier-watch-architecture-per.description: Three-tier watch architecture: per-file (fs.watchFile polling for atomic-write resilience) + per-dir (fs.watch for event-based progress) + periodic interval reconciliation (setInterval safety-net).

debounced-reconciliation-cleartimeout-se.description: Debounced reconciliation (clearTimeout + setTimeout RECONCILE_DEBOUNCE_MS) on top-level fs.watch — prevents thundering-herd reconciles when dashboards/ receives multiple events in a burst.

validate-then-broadcast-every-payload.description: Validate-then-broadcast: every payload runs through isValidInitialization/isValidLogs/isValidProgress; invalid payloads are logged but not broadcast — prevents corrupt state from reaching SSE/IPC consumers.

lazy-progress-watcher-creation-wrapped.description: Lazy progress watcher creation — wrapped in try/catch so a missing progress/ directory doesn't fail the watcher setup (ensureDashboard creates it, but defense in depth).

dependency-aware-emission-status-complet.description: Dependency-aware emission: status==='completed' triggers a delayed computeNewlyUnblocked + 'tasks_unblocked' SSE — the orchestrator (Electron SwarmOrchestrator) listens and dispatches the next wave automatically.

map-based-watcher-registry-dashboardwatc.description: Map-based watcher registry (dashboardWatchers) keyed by id — single source of truth for active watchers, used by reconcileDashboards to compute add/remove deltas.

hard-schema-guards-validateandbroadcast-.description: Hard schema guards in validateAndBroadcast (task_id matches filename, dashboard_id matches directory) — guards against cross-dashboard write contamination, broadcasts 'write_rejected' for UI surfacing. NOTE: this function is currently NOT wired into the live path.

status-aggregation-lane-status-derived.description: Status aggregation: lane.status is derived from task statuses (allCompleted -> completed, anyFailed -> failed, anyInProgress -> in_progress, else pending) inside shapeAgent — single source of truth for the row badge.

bfs-dependency-line-reuse-chat.description: BFS dependency-line reuse: chat dashboard uses the SAME drawDependencyLines/setupCardHoverEffects helpers as the code dashboard, just scoped to a single lane's container/svg.

live-updates-pattern-window-electronapi.description: Live-updates pattern: `window.electronAPI.on('chat_dashboard_changed', () => loadData())`; the cleanup function returned by the IPC bridge MUST be invoked in useEffect cleanup.

graceful-degradation-demo-data-fallback.description: Graceful degradation: demo data is the fallback for both 'no API' and 'API failed' branches — chat dashboard never blanks out.

reuse-prop-same-claudeview-component.description: Reuse-by-prop: same ClaudeView component renders in two surfaces (code-floating-panel and chat-instance) — distinguished only by `tab`/`surface` props, not by a separate component.

pseudo-id-namespace-chat-tabs.description: Pseudo-id namespace: chat tabs masquerade as dashboards via the 'chat-agent-' prefix, letting per-dashboard utilities (project storage, processing-stash, unread counts) work uniformly across both surfaces.

empty-state-stub-pattern-render.description: Empty-state stub pattern: render a low-opacity SVG icon plus heading + subtext, used uniformly across Synapse for placeholder views.

multi-step-modal-flow-refs.description: Multi-step modal flow with refs (pickedProjectRef, projectSavedRef, pendingChatKeyRef) to bridge async user choices into a single finalize call — refs are used because the values must NOT trigger re-renders.

pseudo-id-project-storage-chat.description: Pseudo-id project storage: chat tabs reuse the dashboardProjects localStorage util by passing 'chat-agent-<hex>' (or transient 'pending-chat-<ts>') instead of a real dashboard slug.

status-dot-tri-state-derived.description: Status dot tri-state derived from cross-slice context (currentDashboardId + claudeIsProcessing + claudeProcessingStash + unreadChatCounts).

sidebar-collapse-drives-css-custom.description: Sidebar collapse drives a CSS custom property (--sidebar-width) on document.documentElement so unrelated panels can react without prop drilling.

all-chat-tab-actions-dispatch.description: All chat-tab actions dispatch through AppContext (CHAT_TAB_CREATE / CHAT_TAB_SWITCH / CHAT_TAB_RENAME / CHAT_TAB_DELETE / SET_CHAT_VIEW) — sidebar holds NO chat tab state itself.

status-drives-border-dot-bg.description: Status drives border, dot, and bg color via STATUS_COLORS / STATUS_BG_COLORS lookup with 'pending' fallback.

inline-style-overrides-allow-per.description: Inline style overrides allow per-status border/background while keeping all layout in CSS classes.

click-guard-window-getselection-tostring.description: Click guard `window.getSelection().toString()` prevents accidental modal opens during text selection — also used in ChainPipeline cards.

per-dashboard-isolation-termstate-dashbo.description: Per-dashboard isolation via `termState[dashboardId]` map; effect ensures every dashboardId has a record before tabs render.

all-panes-mounted-only-display.description: All panes mounted; only `display` toggles between flex/none — preserves scroll position and component state across tab switches.

drag-handle-dragstateref-rafref-no.description: Drag handle uses `dragStateRef` + `rafRef` (no React state during drag) to avoid re-render storms; `bottom-panel-resizing` body class disables hover effects across the app while dragging.

lookup-map-agentmap-built-once.description: Lookup map (agentMap) built once per render to avoid quadratic scans inside nested loops.

visibleids-set-computed-once-when.description: VisibleIds Set computed once when filter is active so chain pruning is O(chains).

streaming-state-lives-entirely-refs.description: Streaming state lives entirely in refs; React state updates are dispatched through the context reducer with a functional updater (CLAUDE_UPDATE_MESSAGES + updater fn) to avoid stale closures.

stashing-pattern-when-active-dashboard.description: Stashing pattern: when the active dashboard or tab changes, every relevant ref (activeTaskIdsRef, codexStreamedTaskIdsRef, currentTextIndexRef, toolCallIndexRef) is written to a per-dashboard stash map and re-loaded from the target's stash.

three-routing-paths-worker-output.description: Three routing paths in worker-output: (a) active-dashboard+active-tab → handleChunk/processEvent, (b) active-dashboard+other-tab → routeToTabStash, (c) inactive-dashboard → buffer to dashboard stash slice. Each path duplicates the JSON event parsing.

tool-calls-render-toolcallblock-rich.description: Tool calls render via ToolCallBlock with rich per-tool formatters (Read/Edit/Write/Bash/Grep/Glob/Task/WebFetch/WebSearch/TodoWrite/NotebookEdit) — Edit/Write default to expanded, others collapsed.

askuserquestion-tool-renders-interactive.description: AskUserQuestion tool_use renders an interactive options card; submitting calls back through onSendAnswer→sendText to keep the chat as a normal turn.

conversation-history-injected-only-fresh.description: Conversation history injected only on FRESH sessions (no sessionId) — once a CLI --resume sessionId exists, the CLI owns history; injecting it again would double-context.

pure-presentational-computes-color-strin.description: Pure presentational; computes color string inline from a ternary.

document-level-mousedown-listener-patter.description: Document-level mousedown listener pattern with ref+open-state for outside-click dropdown dismissal — repeated in ModeMenu, QueuePopup, Sidebar, ClaudeView quick-access editor.

electron-only-ui-gated-useiselectron.description: Electron-only UI gated via useIsElectron() so SSE-only browser mode hides Commands.

section-component-homesection-accepts-em.description: Section component (HomeSection) accepts an `empty` string used both as the empty-state copy and as the truthy/falsy gate for rendering children.

status-mapping-helpers-statusdotclass-st.description: Status mapping helpers statusDotClass / statusLabel are defined once at module scope and reused by IdleItem and DashboardCard.

smart-auto-scroll-guard-el.description: Smart auto-scroll guard: `el.scrollHeight - el.scrollTop - el.clientHeight < 40` — same idiom used in ClaudeView (with 80px threshold).

single-pass-count-aggregation-usememo.description: Single-pass count aggregation in useMemo to avoid filtering N times for N levels.

usecallback-wraps-fetchmetrics-so-pollin.description: useCallback wraps fetchMetrics so the polling effect's deps stay stable.

poll-only-when-expanded-closes.description: POLL only when expanded — closes interval in cleanup so collapsed panels don't burn requests.

outside-click-pattern-useref-useeffect.description: Outside-click pattern: useRef + useEffect + addEventListener('mousedown') gated by open state; identical to Header archive dropdown.

conditional-render-expanded-panel-as.description: Conditional render of expanded panel as sibling of FAB inside a single .queue-popup-container.

stoppropagation-fab-click-prevents-docum.description: stopPropagation on FAB click prevents the document listener from immediately collapsing the panel.

usememo-over-ideworkspaces-builds-two.description: useMemo over ideWorkspaces builds two parallel structures (Set + Map) to avoid scanning the array twice during sort.

dashboard-render-keys-off-id.description: Dashboard render keys off id; itemClasses array is filtered+joined to avoid stray spaces.

all-async-electronapi-calls-swallow.description: All async electronAPI calls swallow errors with .catch(()=>{}) at the call site — failures are silent UX-wise.

drag-enter-leave-counter-dragcounterref.description: drag enter/leave uses a counter (dragCounterRef) to handle nested elements correctly — without this, hovering over a child of the drop target fires extra leave events.

computeelapsed-wrapped-usecallback-keep-.description: computeElapsed wrapped in useCallback to keep the effect's deps stable; effect cleans up the interval on completion or unmount.

recalcwaves-agentlist-wavelist-called-ev.description: recalcWaves(agentList, waveList) is called everywhere the agents change to keep wave counts in sync and prune empty waves.

modal-state-lives-component-taskeditorop.description: Modal state lives in this component (taskEditorOpen + editingTask) and the modal is conditionally mounted only when open.

refs-everything-should-not-trigger.description: Refs for everything that should not trigger re-renders: terminalRef, fitAddonRef, terminalIdRef, isDisposedRef, spawnRef.

resizeobserver-tied-container-observe-fi.description: ResizeObserver tied to the container; on observe-fire it fits AND notifies PTY of cols/rows so the shell wraps correctly.

restart-keypress-pattern-terminal-ondata.description: Restart-on-keypress pattern: terminal.onData() handler re-arms only when `exited` becomes true.

status-label-mapping-inline-build.description: Status-to-label mapping inline in the build loop (`a.status === 'failed' ? 'Agent failed' : 'Agent completed'`).

event-keys-combine-timestamp-index.description: Event keys combine timestamp + index to disambiguate co-located events.

two-parallel-maps-built-per.description: Two parallel maps built per render: agentMap (id→agent) and per-wave aggregates (completedByWave, totalByWave) for the header badge.

drawdependencylines-invoked-both-inside-.description: drawDependencyLines is invoked both inside the main effect AND inside the ResizeObserver callback to handle layout shifts after initial draw.

imperative-dom-querying-cards-queryselec.description: Imperative DOM querying for cards (`querySelectorAll('.agent-card[data-agent-id]')`) instead of refs — necessary because cards are rendered dynamically.

local-remote-sections-independent-collap.description: Local + remote sections use independent collapse state (localCollapsed default open, remoteCollapsed default closed).

inline-defined-confirmdialog-mergeprevie.description: Inline-defined ConfirmDialog and MergePreviewDialog (NOT shared with SafetyDialogs.jsx) — closes on Escape via window keydown listener.

merge-action-loads-preview-running.description: Merge action loads a preview by running `gitLog(repoPath, 50, [`${currentBranch}..${sourceBranch}`])` to display the commits that would be merged.

graph-lane-colors-derive-from.description: Graph lane colors derive from getComputedStyle (--color-in-progress) at module load, then use a fixed palette of 10 stable colors keyed by lane index.

operation-errors-show-banner-above.description: Operation errors show in a banner above the lists and auto-clear after 8 seconds.

graphcell-cubic-bezier-curves-draw.description: GraphCell uses cubic-Bezier curves to draw merge connections with 0.4 vertical control offsets — keeps merge curves visually smooth across rows.

three-section-collapsible-layout-chevron.description: Three-section collapsible layout with chevron icons (ChevronIcon) and per-section open state (stagedOpen, unstagedOpen, untrackedOpen).

splitpath-helper-renders-basename-promin.description: splitPath() helper renders the basename prominently with the parent directory in a dim suffix — typical IDE file-list pattern.

all-mutations-flow-through-refreshstatus.description: All mutations flow through refreshStatus which re-fetches gitStatus and dispatches GIT_SET_STATUS to update sibling components (CommitPanel, BranchPanel).

subject-character-counter-color-flips.description: Subject character counter color flips to var(--color-blocked) when subjectLen > 50; body wrap indicator flips when any line exceeds 72.

commit-button-shows-inline-staged.description: Commit button shows an inline staged-count badge (rounded pill with stagedCount) when not amending — visual confirmation of what will be committed.

refresh-after-commit-fires-gitstatus.description: Refresh after commit fires gitStatus + gitLog in parallel via Promise.all — both must complete before onCommitComplete is invoked.

two-column-line-numbers-oldnum.description: Two-column line numbers (oldNum / newNum) + content with prefix + / − / space — classic GitHub-style unified diff.

usememo-both-binary-detection-parsing.description: useMemo for both binary detection and parsing — diff text can be large, recomputing on every render is wasteful.

status-class-map-m-d.description: STATUS_CLASS map (M/A/D/R/U/?/T/C) reused independently of ChangesPanel's STATUS_MAP — kept here so DiffViewer is decoupled.

three-mode-render-gate-hasrepos.description: Three-mode render gate: `!hasRepos` → GitWelcome, `!isGitRepo` → InitFlow, otherwise full layout. Each mode reuses the RepoTabs header.

parallel-data-load-promise-all.description: Parallel data load via Promise.all — five endpoints fire concurrently; each is null-checked in case the API surface evolves.

refresh-callback-refreshgitdata-passed-c.description: Refresh callback (refreshGitData) is passed to CommitPanel onCommitComplete — used to re-fetch everything after a commit lands.

default-sidebar-width-300-min.description: DEFAULT_SIDEBAR_WIDTH (300) / MIN (220) / MAX (500) — clamp logic uses Math.min(MAX, Math.max(MIN, ...)).

empty-state-pattern-git-manager.description: Empty-state pattern: git-manager-empty container + git-manager-empty-icon + title + text + primary action button.

folder-name-extraction-replace-trailing.description: Folder name extraction: replace trailing slashes, split on / or \, take last segment — handles cross-platform paths.

aggressive-lane-consolidation-computegra.description: Aggressive lane consolidation: computeGraphLayout finds ALL lanes that expect a hash and folds duplicates into the lowest lane — keeps the graph compact when branches converge.

three-connection-types-pass-vertical.description: Three connection types: 'pass' (vertical pass-through), 'parent' (down to first parent), 'merge' (curved bezier from secondary parent). Plus 'convergingLines' for freed duplicate lanes at convergence points.

debounced-search-author-filters-ref.description: Debounced search/author filters use ref-tracked setTimeout to avoid firing per keystroke.

expandable-rows-render-commitdetails-sub.description: Expandable rows render a CommitDetails sub-component below the row with metadata, files-changed, and optional on-demand diff.

three-state-ui-idle-initializing.description: Three-state UI: idle/initializing/success — driven by initializing and success flags.

reuses-git-manager-empty-container.description: Reuses git-manager-empty container class for the centered card — same style as GitWelcome.

single-activedialog-state-save-pull.description: Single activeDialog state ('save'|'pull'|'push'|'fetch'|'sync'|'moveBranch'|'stash'|'stashPop'|'undoCommit'|'discardAll') controls which ConfirmDialog renders — mutually exclusive.

dangerlevel-mapping-safe-pull-push.description: Dangerlevel mapping: safe (pull/push/fetch/sync/save/stash/stashPop), warning (undoCommit, moveBranch via inline dialog), danger (discardAll with type-to-confirm).

modal-closes-escape-window-keydown.description: Modal closes on Escape via window keydown listener attached only when modalOpen.

showsuccess-sets-transient-success-messa.description: showSuccess sets a transient success message that auto-clears after 3 seconds — distinct from per-dialog error which lives in `error` state.

result-toast-auto-dismiss-after.description: Result toast with auto-dismiss after 5s — type 'success'|'error'|'warning' maps to color tokens.

anyoperationrunning-pushing-pulling-fetc.description: anyOperationRunning = pushing || pulling || fetching || gitLoading — single boolean disables ALL actions during any in-flight op.

after-every-remote-op-refreshafterremote.description: After every remote op, refreshAfterRemoteOp re-fetches gitStatus + gitLog + gitAheadBehind + gitBranches in parallel.

ahead-behind-chips-render-arrow.description: Ahead/behind chips render with arrow icons (ArrowUpIcon green for ahead, ArrowDownIcon orange for behind), or a CheckIcon Synced badge.

active-state-determined-repo-id.description: Active state determined by `repo.id === gitActiveRepoId` — repo.id is the dispatch key, not the path (paths can be moved or duplicated).

title-attribute-each-tab-shows.description: Title attribute on each tab shows the full path — useful when names truncate.

stoppropagation-close-button-essential-b.description: stopPropagation on the close button is essential because the parent button has its own onClick.

three-tier-danger-level-system.description: Three-tier danger level system with consistent icon + button class mapping — used by QuickActions for all destructive flows.

affecteditems-list-rendered-as-scrollabl.description: AffectedItems list rendered as a scrollable code-style list (git-manager-dialog-affected) — keeps long file lists readable.

two-step-double-confirm-step.description: Two-step double-confirm: step 1 is just acknowledge, step 2 has the type-to-confirm input — the actual onConfirm only fires from step 2.

module-level-breakpoint-state-allbreakpo.description: Module-level breakpoint state (allBreakpoints Map) survives React unmount; component-level refs hold per-mount editor instance + decoration IDs.

debounced-diagnostics-500ms-re-run.description: Debounced diagnostics (500ms) re-run after save (handleSave calls runDiagnostics) and on file load.

theme-rebuilt-from-css-variables.description: Theme is rebuilt from CSS variables and re-applied on documentElement attribute mutations (data-theme/style) — Synapse theme switches propagate to Monaco automatically.

cross-component-navigation-pattern-exter.description: Cross-component navigation pattern: external code dispatches { key:'ideNavigateToLine', value: { filePath, line, column, ts } } → CodeEditor effect compares filePath and reveals/sets position. The `ts` field forces re-trigger on identical line.

dirty-clean-state-dual-tracked.description: Dirty/clean state is dual-tracked: originalContentRef (local, for editor) + IDE_MARK_FILE_DIRTY action (global, for tabs).

severity-glyph-mapping-centralized-sever.description: Severity-to-glyph mapping is centralized via severityToGlyphClass + SEVERITY_MAP for consistency across diagnostics and Problems panel.

push-event-subscription-useeffect-cleanu.description: Push-event subscription in useEffect with cleanup function returned from window.electronAPI.on() — the API returns its own unsubscribe function.

sticky-bottom-auto-scroll-ref.description: Sticky-bottom auto-scroll uses ref (autoScrollRef) instead of state — avoids re-render cascades during rapid output.

command-history-dedup-filter-out.description: Command history dedup: filter out duplicates of new expr first, then append → most recent at index length-1.

type-driven-css-class-type.description: Type-driven CSS class via TYPE_CLASS_MAP lookup (stdout, stderr, log, eval-input/result/error, system).

collapsiblesection-reusable-inner-compon.description: CollapsibleSection is a reusable inner component — used by all four sections for consistent header/chevron/count UI.

variablerow-recursive-renders-itself-eac.description: VariableRow is recursive: renders itself for each child after lazy-loaded expansion; depth controls paddingLeft visually (12 + depth * 16).

empty-disabled-state-dbg-empty.description: Empty/disabled state uses 'dbg-empty' class with messages like 'Not paused', 'No call frames', 'No breakpoints set'.

navigation-callbacks-onnavigatetoframe-o.description: Navigation callbacks (onNavigateToFrame, onNavigateToBreakpoint) are PROP-DRIVEN — the panel itself never dispatches navigation; IDEView wires up the file-open + ideNavigateToLine flow.

pure-prop-component-no-appcontext.description: Pure-prop component — no AppContext, no IPC. Reusable in isolation/storybook.

status-driven-boolean-flags-isidle.description: Status-driven boolean flags (isIdle/isRunning/isPaused/isStopped) computed at top of render, then per-button can* booleans derived.

inline-svg-icons-every-action.description: Inline SVG icons for every action (Play, Pause, StepOver, StepInto, StepOut, Restart, Stop, Launch) — no external icon library.

per-workspace-open-files-state.description: Per-workspace open-files state lives in AppContext (not local) — switching workspaces preserves each one's tabs.

active-tab-ref-callback-auto.description: Active-tab ref callback for auto-scroll-into-view (only set on isActive tab).

dirty-indicator-css-only-dot.description: Dirty indicator is a CSS-only dot via 'editor-tab-dirty' class with bullet character (visual only, not text).

lazy-directory-loading-setloadingpaths-p.description: Lazy directory loading: setLoadingPaths(prev → add) → ideListDir → dispatch IDE_UPDATE_FILE_TREE_NODE → setLoadingPaths(prev → delete).

treenode-recursive-renders-itself-each.description: TreeNode is RECURSIVE — renders itself for each child when expanded.

getfileicon-ext-tolowercase-last-dot.description: getFileIcon uses ext.toLowerCase() of last dot segment; multi-extension files like .test.js resolve as 'js'.

sidebar-view-toggle-dispatch-key.description: Sidebar view toggle via dispatch({ key:'ideSidebarView', value:'search'|'explorer' }) — IDEView swaps SearchPanel ↔ FileExplorer based on this.

multi-effect-debug-ipc-subscription.description: Multi-effect debug IPC subscription pattern: an array of cleanup functions accumulated then iterated on unmount.

draggable-divider-mousedown-document-mou.description: Draggable divider via mousedown→document mousemove/mouseup with ref-stored startX/startWidth.

conditional-rendering-debugtoolbar-debug.description: Conditional rendering of DebugToolbar + DebugPanels only when debugSession.status !== 'idle' — they appear/disappear with the session.

embedded-bottompanel-embedded-prop-both.description: Embedded BottomPanel (`embedded` prop) in both welcome and main layouts.

dual-entry-point-opening-workspaces.description: Dual entry point for opening workspaces: this component (cold-start welcome) and WorkspaceTabs (when workspaces exist). Both should use the same IDE_OPEN_WORKSPACE + createWorkspaceDashboard sequence.

folder-name-extraction-split-pop.description: Folder name extraction via .split(/[/\\]/).pop() — handles both POSIX and Windows.

createworkspacedashboard-call-wrapped-in.description: createWorkspaceDashboard call wrapped in inner try/catch so welcome action doesn't fail if dashboard creation fails.

pure-derivation-from-state-usememo.description: Pure-derivation-from-state via useMemo — no local mutation of diagnostics.

severityicon-severity-driven-svg-red.description: SeverityIcon is severity-driven SVG: red circle for error, yellow triangle for warning, purple circle for info/hint.

filter-toggles-buttons-active-modifier.description: Filter toggles are buttons with 'active' modifier class; clicking flips activeFilters[severity].

debounced-search-input-300ms-enter.description: Debounced search input (300ms) + Enter for immediate execution.

search-options-stored-globally-appcontex.description: Search options stored globally in AppContext (state.ideSearchOptions = { regex, caseSensitive, wholeWord, includeGlob, excludeGlob }) — persistent across panel toggles.

single-source-truth-results-query.description: Single source of truth: results, query, options, replaceMode, replaceText, loading all live in AppContext via IDE_SET_SEARCH_* actions.

back-explorer-button-esc-key.description: 'Back to Explorer' button + ESC key both flip ideSidebarView to 'explorer'.

stateless-presentational-component-recei.description: Stateless presentational component — receives all data via props.

inline-mark-highlighting-picks-up.description: Inline <mark> for highlighting — picks up CSS via .ide-search-match-highlight class.

fileresult-relativepath-optional-falls-b.description: fileResult.relativePath optional — falls back to fileResult.file when missing.

one-shot-mount-time-recovery.description: One-shot mount-time recovery effect via useRef boolean guard — recreates missing dashboards after app restart.

workspace-dashboard-linking-has-three.description: Workspace↔Dashboard linking has THREE storage layers: (1) ideWorkspaceManager localStorage map, (2) ws.dashboardId in React state, (3) saveDashboardProject mapping. All three must agree.

handlecloseworkspace-4-step-ordered-casc.description: handleCloseWorkspace is a 4-step ordered cascade: get mapping → switch away if current → close in state → delete on disk → REMOVE_DASHBOARD action.

every-other-modal-directory-wraps.description: Every other modal in this directory wraps its body in <Modal title=... onClose=... className=...> rather than reimplementing overlay/ESC/header logic — single source of truth for keyboard and click-out behavior.

modals-need-fixed-action-buttons.description: Modals that need fixed action buttons at the bottom use the `footer` prop (e.g. ProjectModal); modals with inline buttons in the body do not (e.g. ConfirmModal, ErrorModal, TaskEditorModal).

inline-statusbadge-sub-component-duplica.description: Inline StatusBadge sub-component is duplicated across AgentDetails.jsx and TaskDetails.jsx — they share visual style but are NOT extracted to a shared module.

wrapper-title-empty-string-because.description: Wrapper title is empty string ('') because this modal renders its own custom header layout (id + title block) instead of using Modal's default title bar.

optional-sections-deps-meta-files.description: Optional sections (deps, meta, files, progress, milestones, deviations, logs) are conditionally rendered with truthy guards — empty data shows nothing rather than empty placeholders.

same-header-comment-notes-mirrors.description: Same header comment notes 'Mirrors ArchiveModal.js' — this is the JSX rewrite of an older vanilla version. Multiple modal files carry this 'Mirrors X.js' note (HistoryModal, SettingsModal, TaskDetails).

optimistic-ui-setarchives-prev-filter.description: Optimistic UI: setArchives prev.filter(...) is called before any server confirmation in fallback path — diverges from convention where server confirms first.

multi-scope-sidebar-collapsible-folders.description: Multi-scope sidebar with collapsible folders and per-folder Add (+) buttons — same expand/collapse pattern as IDE FileExplorer but lighter weight.

viewer-state-machine-placeholder-view.description: Viewer state machine ('placeholder'|'view'|'edit'|'generate') drives which sub-component renders — clean state isolation between view/edit/generate flows.

user-can-choose-between-bare.description: User can choose between bare-template manual creation (CommandEditor with COMMAND_TEMPLATE) and AI-generated rich command (CommandGenerator).

buttons-follow-naming-convention-modal.description: Buttons follow naming convention .modal-btn + variant suffix (.modal-btn-cancel, .modal-btn-confirm, .modal-btn-close).

companion-errormodal-both-minimal-wrappe.description: Companion to ErrorModal — both are minimal wrappers over Modal with stylized actions.

same-minimal-wrapping-pattern-as.description: Same minimal wrapping pattern as ConfirmModal — Modal + message paragraph + .modal-actions row.

color-failed-css-variable-rather.description: Uses --color-failed CSS variable rather than a hardcoded red — themable across all 6 theme presets.

header-comment-mirrors-historymodal-js.description: Header comment 'Mirrors HistoryModal.js' — JSX-converted from older vanilla.

three-sub-components-analyticssection-to.description: Three sub-components: AnalyticsSection (top), HistoryEntry (per row) — keeps render concerns separated.

promise-all-loads-history-analytics.description: Promise.all loads history + analytics in parallel.

usememo-wraps-filter-counts-avoid.description: useMemo wraps filter and counts to avoid re-computation on every render.

prevlengthref-pattern-vs-length-as.description: prevLengthRef pattern (vs length-as-state) prevents render-cycle dependency on prev length.

data-level-attribute-filter-buttons.description: data-level attribute on filter buttons enables CSS to color buttons per level (vs inline style).

single-component-handles-two-distinct.description: Single component handles two distinct modes via a boolean prop — simpler than two separate components but increases prop API surface.

unicode-escapes-icon-u26a0-warning.description: Uses unicode escapes for icon (\u26A0 = warning triangle, \u2192 = right arrow) rather than emoji — consistent ASCII rendering across platforms.

provider-agnostic-agent-spawn-settings.description: Provider-agnostic agent spawn: settings.agentProvider toggles between claude/codex, settings.{provider}CliPath supplies binary path. Same pattern in ProjectModal, ChatInstanceView, etc.

tri-format-stream-json-parser.description: Tri-format stream-json parser (direct/NDJSON/result) — robust to streaming variants from Claude SDK, Claude CLI, and Codex.

useref-listener-unsub-functions-accumula.description: useRef for listener-unsub functions and accumulated output, useState for reactive UI flags — splits mutable refs from rendered state correctly.

dashboard-scoped-settings-localstorage-d.description: Dashboard-scoped settings via localStorage (dashboardProjects helpers) — same pattern used by ChatInstanceView and PlanningModal for additional-context dirs.

three-tier-project-resolution-pristine.description: Three-tier project resolution: pristine override → per-dashboard → global fallback (with chat exception).

per-provider-sub-state-clipath.description: Per-provider sub-state: cliPath, defaultModel, MODEL_OPTIONS keyed by provider — clean swap when provider changes.

theme-system-6-named-themes.description: Theme system: 6 named themes via [data-theme=name] CSS rules + 1 custom theme via inline style props on documentElement.

theme-constants-themes-default-custom.description: Theme constants (THEMES, DEFAULT_CUSTOM_COLORS, COLOR_FIELDS, APP_FIELDS) declared at module top — all data-driven so the grid auto-renders.

update-state-subscription-api-update.description: Update state subscription via api.on('update-status', ...) — live progress percentage during downloads.

header-comment-mirrors-taskdetailsmodal-.description: Header comment 'Mirrors TaskDetailsModal.js' — JSX rewrite of vanilla.

custom-header-inside-body-rather.description: Custom header inside body rather than using Modal title — gives more typographic control.

conditional-ui-new-wave-option.description: Conditional UI: '+ New Wave' option in select toggles a follow-up name input field — common pattern for inline-creation in dropdowns.

boolean-isedit-derived-from-edittask.description: Boolean isEdit derived from !!editTask drives initial state values.

stream-json-event-router-processevent.description: Stream-JSON event router (processEvent switch) with provider parity (Claude SDK + Codex stream-json) — same parsing strategy used in PlanningModal.extractJSON.

tool-call-tool-result-correlation.description: Tool call + tool result correlation via id-to-index map — enables collapsed result expansion without parent re-render.

handlechunkref-current-handlechunk-patte.description: handleChunkRef.current = handleChunk pattern — workaround for IPC listener closure capturing stale state.

view-router-switch-activeview-rendermain.description: View-router uses switch on activeView in renderMainContent / renderChatContent — do NOT add new views via conditional && chains, follow the switch pattern.

modal-dispatch-type-open-modal.description: Modal dispatch uses { type: 'OPEN_MODAL', modal, dashboardId } and { type: 'CLOSE_MODAL' } — modal name (not boolean) drives which modal renders; activeModal is single-string-or-null in state.

progresssection-extracts-active-task-fro.description: ProgressSection extracts active_task from currentStatus; it computes display values inline rather than receiving props because mergeState in useDashboardData already populates totals.

cleardashboardsection-archives-before-cl.description: ClearDashboardSection archives BEFORE clearing — archiveDashboard then clearDashboard chained via .catch(() => {}) — failures swallowed silently.

project-name-shown-action-bar.description: Project name shown in action bar is derived by stripping trailing slashes and pop()-ing last path segment — same algorithm used in ClaudeFloatingHeader.

replanning-banner-detection-lives-merges.description: Replanning banner detection lives in mergeState (useDashboardData), not here — App just renders if task.overall_status === 'replanning'.

fetch-interception-window-fetch-reassign.description: Fetch interception via window.fetch reassignment guarded by window.electronAPI presence — same pattern works for any Electron-React app needing browser-style fetch over IPC.

url-pattern-matching-regex-e.description: URL pattern matching via regex (e.g. /^\/api\/dashboards\/([^/]+)\/(.+)$/) — extend by adding a new regex match in routeToIPC.

createroot-render-single-nested-provider.description: createRoot + .render with single nested provider — no React.StrictMode wrapper (intentional given IPC listener side-effects).

surface-aware-slice-routing-action.description: Surface-aware slice routing: action.surface ('code'|'chat') + fieldsFor(surface) + SLICE_FIELDS lookup table — adopt this pattern when adding any chat field.

context-split-performance-appcontext-sta.description: Context split for performance: AppContext (state) + DispatchContext (dispatch) — components needing only dispatch import only useDispatch.

action-shape-type-namespace-action.description: Action shape: { type: 'NAMESPACE_ACTION', ...payload } — payload keys vary per action; switch cases destructure them inline.

persistence-helpers-loadsaved-save-live.description: Persistence helpers (loadSaved*, save*) live in the file with try/catch around localStorage to handle quota/private-browsing — never let a localStorage failure crash the app.

tab-stash-key-pattern-dashboardid.description: Tab stash key pattern: dashboardId + ':' + tabId — used as both localStorage key suffix and in-memory tabStash key.

initial-state-bootstrap-reads-localstora.description: Initial state bootstrap reads localStorage synchronously at module-load time (savedWorkspaces, savedGitRepos, savedChatTabs, etc.) — first-render shows persisted state.

useref-false-useeffect-one-shot.description: useRef(false) + useEffect for one-shot migrations (backfilledRef pattern) — ensures async migration runs exactly once across re-renders.

cache-ref-pattern-progressref-current.description: Cache + ref pattern: progressRef.current[dashboardId] = ... lets stale-closure-safe IPC handlers read latest cache without depending on React state.

mergestate-pure-init-progress-logs.description: mergeState is pure (init+progress+logs in, status object out) — easy to test and reuse outside the hook.

listener-cleanup-listenersref-current-ar.description: Listener cleanup uses listenersRef.current array of {channel, handle} — handle is the function returned by api.on(), called to unsubscribe.

health-check-interval-polling-lasteventt.description: Health-check via interval polling lastEventTime — preferred over connection events from main process which are not pushed.

re-merge-effect-useeffect-deps.description: Re-merge effect: useEffect with deps [currentInit, currentProgress, currentLogs] dispatches SET_STATUS — keeps currentStatus derived but in-store for cheap component reads.

usememo-empty-deps-capture-window.description: useMemo with empty deps to capture window globals — same pattern useful for any window.X bridge.

direct-style-mutation-inside-raf.description: Direct style.* mutation inside rAF for drag/resize — avoids React re-render thrashing and keeps drags at native frame rate.

pointer-events-setpointercapture-more-ro.description: Pointer events + setPointerCapture is more robust than mouse events for window-leaving drags — use this pattern for any custom drag UI.

data-resize-edge-attribute-selector.description: data-resize-edge attribute selector for delegated handle detection — handles are siblings inside the panel; click on any handle reads its edge from dataset.

iife-strict-mode-idempotent-guard.description: IIFE with strict mode and idempotent guard — required for any executeJavaScript-injected script.

css-custom-properties-theme-variables.description: CSS custom properties for theme variables inside injected styles — avoids hardcoded colors scattered across selectors.

mutationobserver-debounced-scan-canonica.description: MutationObserver + debounced scan — the canonical SPA-aware DOM-watching pattern.

postmessage-structured-type-field-host.description: postMessage with structured type field — host filters by message.data.type === 'synapse-edit'.

theme-tokens-read-from-css.description: Theme tokens read from CSS custom properties via getComputedStyle(documentElement).getPropertyValue — gives JS access to CSS-defined theme without duplication.

mutable-constant-maps-initialized-once.description: Mutable constant maps initialized once per app boot — preferred over re-deriving on every component render.

pure-localstorage-helpers-no-react.description: Pure localStorage helpers — no React state. Components call these directly and re-render via dispatch when they care.

disk-sync-as-opt-side.description: Disk-sync as opt-in side-effect (only when window.electronAPI.saveAdditionalContext exists) — keeps utility usable in standalone-server mode.

bfs-over-sparse-pathway-grid.description: BFS over a sparse pathway grid for non-overlapping line routing — avoids the visual mess of straight-through lines on dense pipelines.

svg-separate-visible-polyline-transparen.description: SVG with separate visible polyline + transparent hit-area polyline for hover detection — wide hit zone, thin visible line.

module-scoped-cache-expensive-computatio.description: Module-scoped cache for expensive computation (grid + paths) — invalidated by layout-rect change-key.

event-delegation-capture-phase-single.description: Event delegation via capture phase — single listener on container handles all card hover events.

pure-functions-no-react-no.description: Pure functions — no React, no state — usable inside render loops freely.

flight-promise-map-pattern-map.description: In-flight promise map pattern — Map<key, Promise> deduplicates concurrent async creators. Reusable for any async-create-once workflow.

pure-localstorage-helpers-async-create.description: Pure-localStorage helpers + async create-with-IPC pattern — same shape as dashboardProjects.js.

placeholder-restore-pattern-fenced-code.description: Placeholder + restore pattern (fenced code blocks → \x00CODE_BLOCK_N\x00 → restored at end) — keeps protected content untouched by intermediate transforms.

block-level-processing-line-line.description: Block-level processing line-by-line with i counter (manual incrementing) — allows lookahead (table separator) and multi-line consumption.

html-escape-before-applying-tags.description: HTML-escape before applying tags so output is XSS-safe — the inverse order would produce bypasses.

vite-worker-import-returns-worker.description: Vite ?worker import returns a Worker constructor — `new editorWorker()` spawns an instance.

self-monacoenvironment-global-registrati.description: self.MonacoEnvironment global registration — required hook for Monaco to find its workers.

inline-overlay-bridge-injection-wv.description: Inline overlay/bridge injection via wv.executeJavaScript after did-finish-load; overlayInjectedRef gates duplicate injection per-page.

cross-isolation-message-passing-window.description: Cross-isolation message passing: window.postMessage in webview -> console.log with __SYNAPSE_EDIT__ prefix -> host console-message listener parses JSON.

url-normalization-heuristic-localhost-ip.description: URL normalization heuristic: localhost/IP -> http://, anything else -> https://.

toolbar-shared-between-empty-state.description: Toolbar shared between empty-state and loaded-state via toolbar JSX variable.

design-tokens-css-custom-properties.description: Design tokens via CSS custom properties on :root.

glass-surfaces-rgba-255-255.description: Glass surfaces: rgba(255,255,255,0.04) on dark bg with subtle border rgba(255,255,255,0.08).

section-banner-comments-section-name.description: Section banner comments: /* ============ Section Name ============ */ for top-level navigation.

status-color-naming-color-state.description: Status color naming: --color-{state} for badges/dots, paired with --color-{state}-bg for tinted backgrounds.

gradient-header-glow-after-pseudo.description: Gradient header glow via ::after pseudo-element with linear-gradient + filter: blur.

heavy-var-token-fallback-pattern.description: Heavy use of var(--token, fallback) pattern for graceful degradation.

toolbar-min-height-height-flex.description: Toolbar uses min-height + height + flex-shrink:0 to lock its size.

loading-bar-absolutely-positioned-within.description: Loading bar absolutely positioned within URL form with translateX keyframe animation.

strict-prefix-scoping-git-manager.description: Strict prefix scoping (.git-manager-*) to enable safe coexistence with other feature stylesheets.

layout-flex-containers-fixed-heights.description: Layout via flex containers with fixed heights and flex-shrink:0 lock-zones.

glass-surfaces-using-surface-border.description: Glass surfaces using --surface and --border tokens consumed from index.css.

structural-container-pattern-100-width.description: Structural container pattern: 100% width/height flex column, overflow:hidden to clip children.

fixed-height-bars-36px-min.description: Fixed-height bars (36px) with min-height + height + flex-shrink:0 lock zones.

pinned-right-buttons-margin-left.description: Pinned-right buttons via margin-left:auto inside flex row.

active-state-applied-sidebar-tab.description: Active state applied via .sidebar-tab.active conditional class.

action-buttons-sized-22x22-slightly.description: Action buttons sized 22x22 (slightly smaller than nav buttons elsewhere — IDE compactness convention).

uppercase-letter-spaced-0-06em.description: Uppercase letter-spaced 0.06em titles for section headers.

tab-top-accent-pseudo-element.description: Tab top-accent via pseudo-element rather than border-top to avoid layout shift.

focus-visible-outline-offset-2px.description: Focus-visible outline-offset: -2px to inset focus ring.

uppercase-0-06em-letter-spaced.description: Uppercase 0.06em letter-spaced section titles consistent with ide-explorer.css.

inline-match-highlights-styled-span.description: Inline match highlights via styled <span> wrappers in result text.

section-banner-navigation-each-section.description: Section banner navigation: each section starts with /* ============ Title ============ */ banner.

mix-theme-variables-hard-coded.description: Mix of theme variables and hard-coded VS Code dark palette.

monospace-font-stack-cascadia-code.description: Monospace font stack 'Cascadia Code', 'Fira Code', 'Consolas' for REPL feel.

custom-thin-scrollbar-scrollbar-width.description: Custom thin scrollbar via scrollbar-width:thin + scrollbar-color combo.

collapsible-section-pattern-header-actin.description: Collapsible section pattern with header acting as toggle.

empty-state-flex-center-min.description: Empty state: flex center with min-height:60px and muted icon.

Failed worker progress files written by master use status='failed', stage='failed', and extract ERRORS section into a 'level: error' log entry.
task object fields: name (kebab-case slug), type ('Waves'|'Chains'), directory, prompt, project, project_root (absolute), created (ISO 8601 immutable), total_tasks, total_waves.
agents[] fields: id ('{wave}.{index}' or '{wave}.{index}r'), title (~40 char verb phrase), wave (matches waves[].id), layer (optional category), directory (optional), depends_on (string[]).
waves[] fields: id, name (descriptive — UI prepends 'Wave {id}: '), total (count of agents in wave).
chains[] required when task.type='Chains': id, name, tasks[] (ordered agent IDs, each agent in exactly one chain).
Principle 2 — Dependency-driven dispatch, not wave-driven. Waves are visual, dependencies are constraint.
Principle 3 — Pipeline must flow continuously. Record completion -> immediate scan -> dispatch every unblocked task in same cycle.
Principle 4 — No artificial concurrency cap. Send as many agents as there are ready tasks. Bottleneck should be dependencies, not arbitrary limits.

Repair worker dispatch prompt MUST include: failed task's original prompt, failed progress file contents, error description, repair ID and progress file path, instruction to follow failed_task.md (planning-first protocol).
task.type: exact string match 'Waves' or 'Chains'.
agents[].title: short verb phrase, ~40 chars max.
waves[].name: descriptive (UI prepends 'Wave {id}: ' automatically) — not just 'Wave 1'.
chains[].tasks: ordered array of agent IDs left-to-right tracing the dependency path.
history[]: empty array at creation — populated by archive-move, not at write time.
PKI knowledge block format: GOTCHAS / PATTERNS / CONVENTIONS / STALE sections with [file_path] prefix per entry.
Skip PKI when: manifest doesn't exist, empty PKI, no domains/tags match, all matched annotations stale, pure CREATE task with no existing files, or CLAUDE.md alone fills the 200-line budget.
Log every PKI decision: matched domains/tags, file count, annotation reads, staleness warnings — makes augmented planning auditable.

Forbidden master actions: edit application source (.ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx/.py/.go/.rs/.java/.css/.html/.json — except dashboard files), create application files, run application commands (npm run build, npm test, etc.), 'help' a worker by doing part of its task, implement 'just one thing' for speed.
Dashboard files allowed for master: plan.json, initialization.json, logs.json, master_state.json, metrics.json — all under {tracker_root}/dashboards/{id}/.
Five responsibilities are the exhaustive job description — nothing more.
Planning is where the master earns value — invest heavily, never rush.
Wave column title format: 'Wave {id}: {name}' — UI auto-prepends 'Wave {id}: ', so name should be descriptive ('Foundation', 'Auth Layer'), not 'Wave 1'.
TEMPLATE_VERSION 'p_track_v2' for full mode, 'p_v2' for lightweight.

Critical sections never cut for space: SUCCESS CRITERIA, CRITICAL, gotchas (PKI). Cut REFERENCE CODE and CONVENTIONS first.

error_handling.description: Log levels: info, warn, error, debug, permission, deviation — with permission and deviation triggering distinct UI behaviors.

metrics.json schema: swarm_name, computed_at, elapsed_seconds, serial_estimate_seconds, parallel_efficiency, duration_distribution{min,avg,max,median}, failure_rate, max_concurrent, deviation_count, total_tasks, completed_tasks, failed_tasks.
Log metrics summary line format: 'Metrics: {N}s elapsed, {X}x efficiency, {N} max concurrent, {X} failure rate'.
Archive before clear: copy entire dashboard dir to {tracker_root}/Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/ before resetting state — applies in BOTH modes, no exceptions.
logs.json !p entries: only init, per-task completion, per-task failure, and swarm-complete summary entries are written.
Failed worker progress files written by master use status='failed', stage='failed', and extract ERRORS section into a 'level: error' log entry.

logs.json entry fields: timestamp (ISO 8601), task_id ('0.0' for orchestrator else '{wave}.{idx}'), agent ('Orchestrator' or 'Agent N'), level (info|warn|error|debug|permission|deviation), message, task_name.
Permission popup trigger: write log entry with level='permission' BEFORE asking in terminal.
Principle 1 — Always parallelize independent work. Sequential execution of independent tasks is a failure mode.
Repair task ID convention: '{failed_wave}.{next_index}r' (e.g., 2.4r for repair of failed task in wave 2 with 3 existing tasks).

Repair worker dispatch prompt MUST include: failed task's original prompt, failed progress file contents, error description, repair ID and progress file path, instruction to follow failed_task.md (planning-first protocol).
Major-deviation gate: if repair worker discovers fundamental plan error / missing dependency / scope change, it reports back and master writes 'permission' log entry asking user before continuing.
Principle 5 — Errors don't stop the swarm. A failed task blocks only its direct dependents; everything else continues.
Replanning log format: 'Replanning complete — modified: {N}, added: {N}, removed: {N}, retry: {N}. Resuming dispatch.'
PKI knowledge block format: GOTCHAS / PATTERNS / CONVENTIONS / STALE sections with [file_path] prefix per entry.
Stats bar 6 cards: Total (white from task.total_tasks), Completed (green), In Progress (purple), Failed (red when >0), Pending (dim gray, derived), Elapsed (purple while running).
Permission log entry: timestamp, task_id='0.0', agent='Orchestrator', level='permission', message (verbatim popup text), task_name.
Worker progress file mandatory writes: (1) before starting work, (2) on every stage transition, (3) on any deviation, (4) on completion/failure.
Stages enum: reading_context | planning | implementing | testing | finalizing | completed | failed.
Deviation entry shape: { at: ISO8601, severity: 'CRITICAL'|'MODERATE'|'MINOR', description: string }
Log entry shape: { at, level, msg }; milestone shape: { at, msg }; deviation shape: { at, severity, description }.
files_changed entry: { path, action: 'created'|'modified'|'deleted' } with paths relative to {project_root}.
Both 'completed' and 'failed' STATUS values lowercase; severity values uppercase (CRITICAL/MODERATE/MINOR).
